project_title: Ionically Conductive Polymeric Biomaterials and Grafts for Nerve Regeneration
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeEvery year over half a million Americans suffer from large-gap peripheral nerve injuries that require surgicaltreatments with neural grafts or engineered graft substitutes but these treatments suffer from limited availabilityand efficacy. Our objective is to address these issues by developing novel ionically conductive biodegradablescaffolds for reliable electrical and chemical stimulation to enhance the regeneration of large-gap nerve injuries.By bridging the knowledge gap between how chemical and electrical stimulation influence cell-materialinteractions and poorly studied nerve regeneration pathways this project has the potential to significantlyenhance large-gap nerve regeneration and functional recovery ultimately benefitting patients suffering fromperipheral nerve injuries.

project_title: Enhanced Delivery of Cytotoxic Neural Stem Cells for Treatment of Glioblastoma Multiforme Using an Innovative In-situ Thermogelling Hydrogel and Focused Ultrasound
public_health_relevance: RELEVANCE TO PUBLIC HEALTHThe benefit of effective and less invasive therapies that can replace invasive surgical procedures and enhancetreatment outcomes for glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) patients is significant. Biomaterial formulations providingsteady and prolonged release over several months of highly effective tumor homing killer cells known as neuralstem cells (NSCs) and the use of a non-invasive dosing method known as focused ultrasound (FUS) to deliverNSCs systemically and across the blood brain barrier (BBB) can potentially increase efficacy outcomes to GBMtreatment regimen and transform the GBM treatment landscape. The overarching goal of this proposal is todevelop and evaluate a new treatment modality that combines a biodegradable and highly tunable biomaterialscaffold and non-invasive FUS to enhance delivery of NSCs to tumor cells and to curb the treatment of GBM.

project_title: Novel tunable metformin-releasing injectable scaffolds to enhance dental pulp stem cell-based craniofacial bone regeneration
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEPublic Health Relevance: The limited intrinsic regenerative potential of large craniofacial bone defects represents aconsiderable challenge in oral and maxillofacial surgery. The long-term goal of this proposal is to develop novel tissueengineering platforms where mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) with functional organic cation transporters belonging tothe solute carrier 22A gene family can be encapsulated within tunable metformin-releasing injectable scaffolds topotentiate regeneration of vascularized critical-sized bone defects. We anticipate this innovative project will lead tobroader metformin-releasing tissue engineering platforms to benefit many patients in need of oral craniofacial as wellas orthopedic bone regenerative procedures.

project_title: Predictive Drug Release from a Tunable Injectable Capsule
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEMany chronic conditions including age-related macular degeneration rely on frequent local injections for diseasemanagement which are associated with barriers to care including patient discomfort high cost and adverseevents. The overall objective of this project is to develop and validate an injectable biodegradable microcapsulethat can be tuned to extend release of therapeutics of varying sizes including anti-VEGF and triamcinoloneacetonide at least 12 months thereby reducing the need for frequent injections. Theoretical modeling will becombined with in vitro and in vivo experimental studies to optimize the microcapsule system and predicttherapeutic release.

project_title: Rejuvenating aged bone regeneration by innovative nanomaterials-mediated drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE (RELEVANCE)Repair of large bone defects is a crucial medical challenge and tissue engineering (either usingstem/progenitor cells transplantation and/or growth factors e.g. BMPs) is a promisingalternative to autologous bone graft while a number of crucial barriers must be overcome for fulltherapeutic translation. Through a combinatorial approach utilizing small molecule drugs andtunable and biomimetic scaffolds this study will develop novel regenerating therapies fortreatment of bone defects. This work will not only advance knowledge with regard toregenerative medicine but accelerate novel strategies for bone repair/regeneration in a range ofpatients most particularly the elderly.

public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEPublic Health Relevance: The limited intrinsic regenerative potential of large craniofacial bone defects represents aconsiderable challenge in oral and maxillofacial surgery. The long-term goal of this proposal is to develop novel tissueengineering platforms where mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs) with functional organic cation transporters belonging tothe solute carrier 22A gene family can be encapsulated within tunable metformin-releasing injectable scaffolds topotentiate regeneration of vascularized critical-sized bone defects. We anticipate this innovative project will lead tobroader metformin-releasing tissue engineering platforms to benefit many patients in need of oral craniofacial as wellas orthopedic bone regenerative procedures.

project_title: Ionically Conductive Polymeric Biomaterials and Grafts for Nerve Regeneration
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeEvery year over half a million Americans suffer from large-gap peripheral nerve injuries that require surgicaltreatments with neural grafts or engineered graft substitutes but these treatments suffer from limited availabilityand efficacy. Our objective is to address these issues by developing novel ionically conductive biodegradablescaffolds for reliable electrical and chemical stimulation to enhance the regeneration of large-gap nerve injuries.By bridging the knowledge gap between how chemical and electrical stimulation influence cell-materialinteractions and poorly studied nerve regeneration pathways this project has the potential to significantlyenhance large-gap nerve regeneration and functional recovery ultimately benefitting patients suffering fromperipheral nerve injuries.

project_title: Cationic CAMKIIN nanoparticles that reduce chlorine-induced airway oxidative stress
public_health_relevance: PHS StatementChlorine is a chemical agent that is harmful to humans. Inhalation of chlorine creates reactiveoxygen species (ROS) in the lung that cause airway remodeling fibrosis and hyper-reactivity.This proposal will develop a CaMKIIN loaded nanoparticle based therapy that can rapidlymitigate the ROS species in the lung following chlorine exposure.

project_title: Rejuvenating aged bone regeneration by innovative nanomaterials-mediated drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE (RELEVANCE)Repair of large bone defects is a crucial medical challenge and tissue engineering (either usingstem/progenitor cells transplantation and/or growth factors e.g. BMPs) is a promisingalternative to autologous bone graft while a number of crucial barriers must be overcome for fulltherapeutic translation. Through a combinatorial approach utilizing small molecule drugs andtunable and biomimetic scaffolds this study will develop novel regenerating therapies fortreatment of bone defects. This work will not only advance knowledge with regard toregenerative medicine but accelerate novel strategies for bone repair/regeneration in a range ofpatients most particularly the elderly.

project_title: Motorized delivery of bacterial antigens to mucosal barriers to enhance immunity against infection
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThe increasing use of antibiotics has led to a rise in the incidence of Clostridium difficile infections and effectiveways of managing this major public health threat are highly sought after. The goal of this proposal is to developa biomimetic micromotor-based vaccine that is capable of actively delivering bacterial antigens in thegastrointestinal tract to elicit protective immunity. The success of this project will help to establish activepropulsion as an important oral vaccine design parameter which could represent a paradigm-changing conceptthat can be applied to the development of future vaccines against diseases such as bacteria viruses and cancer.

project_title: Stabilization of therapeutic microbes for inclusion in a liquid matrix for delivery to the intestines
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal involves optimizing a drug delivery platform for stabilizing probiotics in a liquidfor inclusion in an enteral feeding (EF) nutritional formula. Providing a complete nutritionalsupplement for EF applications inclusive of a probiotic will help maintain normal gut flora mayincrease antimicrobial/immunological activity suppress the colonization of the gut byenteropathogens reduce diarrhea and assist in colonic water reabsorption due to presence ofshort-chain fatty acids. Furthermore studies have shown significant medical benefits by addingprobiotics to EF formulas including a faster return to normal gut function reduced time on aventilator and reduced ICU hospital stays.

project_title: Predictive Drug Release from a Tunable Injectable Capsule
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEMany chronic conditions including age-related macular degeneration rely on frequent local injections for diseasemanagement which are associated with barriers to care including patient discomfort high cost and adverseevents. The overall objective of this project is to develop and validate an injectable biodegradable microcapsulethat can be tuned to extend release of therapeutics of varying sizes including anti-VEGF and triamcinoloneacetonide at least 12 months thereby reducing the need for frequent injections. Theoretical modeling will becombined with in vitro and in vivo experimental studies to optimize the microcapsule system and predicttherapeutic release.

public_health_relevance: PHS StatementChlorine is a chemical agent that is harmful to humans. Inhalation of chlorine creates reactiveoxygen species (ROS) in the lung that cause airway remodeling fibrosis and hyper-reactivity.This proposal will develop a CaMKIIN loaded nanoparticle based therapy that can rapidlymitigate the ROS species in the lung following chlorine exposure.

project_title: Rejuvenating aged bone regeneration by innovative nanomaterials-mediated drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE (RELEVANCE)Repair of large bone defects is a crucial medical challenge and tissue engineering (either usingstem/progenitor cells transplantation and/or growth factors e.g. BMPs) is a promisingalternative to autologous bone graft while a number of crucial barriers must be overcome for fulltherapeutic translation. Through a combinatorial approach utilizing small molecule drugs andtunable and biomimetic scaffolds this study will develop novel regenerating therapies fortreatment of bone defects. This work will not only advance knowledge with regard toregenerative medicine but accelerate novel strategies for bone repair/regeneration in a range ofpatients most particularly the elderly.

project_title: Motorized delivery of bacterial antigens to mucosal barriers to enhance immunity against infection
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThe increasing use of antibiotics has led to a rise in the incidence of Clostridium difficile infections and effectiveways of managing this major public health threat are highly sought after. The goal of this proposal is to developa biomimetic micromotor-based vaccine that is capable of actively delivering bacterial antigens in thegastrointestinal tract to elicit protective immunity. The success of this project will help to establish activepropulsion as an important oral vaccine design parameter which could represent a paradigm-changing conceptthat can be applied to the development of future vaccines against diseases such as bacteria viruses and cancer.

project_title: Nanoparticle-mediated targeting of hepatic macrophages to mitigate inflammation in alcoholic liver disease
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEChronic alcohol abuse can lead to liver inflammation fibrosis cirrhosis and eventually hepatocellular carcinoma.Current therapies are inadequate in terms of safety and therapeutic efficacy. Our goal is to develop an innovativenanoparticle formulation tagged with a dual-functional ligand for the targeted activation of bile acid receptors andsite-specific release of anti-inflammatory therapeutics for combination therapy to alleviate chronic liverinflammation.

project_title: Microparticle Therapy for Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEOur goal is to develop a therapy for the microvasculopathy Cerebral Amyloid Angiopathy (CAA) based onintranasal delivery of nanoparticles carrying a gene for the complement-regulator CD59. There is increasingevidence that a complement-mediated attack on brain microvascular cells plays a significant role in thepathogenesis of CAA. Optimization of our chitosan-mediated gene delivery system and determination of theCD59 protective effect will be studied in vivo in a relevant CAA mouse model.

project_title: Iron oxide nanotheranostics for breast cancer
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal describes a nanotechnology-based approach that combines the gene and immune therapies totreat breast cancer by simultaneously inhibiting tumor growth and activating T cells for tumor tissuedestruction. Our approach involves the development of a non-viral nanovector that is able to overcome extra-and intra-cellular barriers and specifically deliver genes and induce gene expression only in breast cancercells. This synergetic strategy aims to address the limitations of current breast cancer therapies and ifsuccessful could substantially improve the treatment outcome and minimize deleterious side effects.

project_title: Development of synergistic oxygenating antibacterial hydrogel dressings for  reducing infection in diabetic wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThe primary goals of this R21 re-submission proposal are to better understand the benefit of local supplementaloxygen via our oxygenating hydrogels in reducing infection in diabetic infected wounds and to develop alocalized synergistic oxygenating biomaterial-based wound dressing for controlled antibiotic delivery to helpreducing infection and improve the overall wound healing process. This work will significantly improveunderstanding of fundamental mechanisms leading to reduced infection and reveal the benefits of supplementaloxygen at molecular levels to accelerate development of infected wound healing treatments. Furthermore thiswork is relevant to public health due to its potential to create new biomaterial-based oxygen therapies for diabeticinfected wounds with broad reach.

project_title: Tumor casting hydrogels for interventional radiology treatment of hepatocellular carcinoma
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCECurrent methods for treating a common form of liver cancer (hepatocellular carcinoma) result in poor outcomesfor these patients i.e. only 27% survival after 5 years. We propose to develop a drug-loaded thermosensitivehydrogel that can be injected into the tumor shutting off blood flow and locally delivering drug. We anticipatethat this treatment will result in improved survival for liver cancer patients.

project_title: Rejuvenating aged bone regeneration by innovative nanomaterials-mediated drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE (RELEVANCE)Repair of large bone defects is a crucial medical challenge and tissue engineering (either usingstem/progenitor cells transplantation and/or growth factors e.g. BMPs) is a promisingalternative to autologous bone graft while a number of crucial barriers must be overcome for fulltherapeutic translation. Through a combinatorial approach utilizing small molecule drugs andtunable and biomimetic scaffolds this study will develop novel regenerating therapies fortreatment of bone defects. This work will not only advance knowledge with regard toregenerative medicine but accelerate novel strategies for bone repair/regeneration in a range ofpatients most particularly the elderly.

project_title: Development of synergistic oxygenating antibacterial hydrogel dressings for  reducing infection in diabetic wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThe primary goals of this R21 re-submission proposal are to better understand the benefit of local supplementaloxygen via our oxygenating hydrogels in reducing infection in diabetic infected wounds and to develop alocalized synergistic oxygenating biomaterial-based wound dressing for controlled antibiotic delivery to helpreducing infection and improve the overall wound healing process. This work will significantly improveunderstanding of fundamental mechanisms leading to reduced infection and reveal the benefits of supplementaloxygen at molecular levels to accelerate development of infected wound healing treatments. Furthermore thiswork is relevant to public health due to its potential to create new biomaterial-based oxygen therapies for diabeticinfected wounds with broad reach.

project_title: Iron oxide nanotheranostics for breast cancer
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal describes a nanotechnology-based approach that combines the gene and immune therapies totreat breast cancer by simultaneously inhibiting tumor growth and activating T cells for tumor tissuedestruction. Our approach involves the development of a non-viral nanovector that is able to overcome extra-and intra-cellular barriers and specifically deliver genes and induce gene expression only in breast cancercells. This synergetic strategy aims to address the limitations of current breast cancer therapies and ifsuccessful could substantially improve the treatment outcome and minimize deleterious side effects.

project_title: Novel Therapeutic Approaches For Treatment of Intestinal Inflammation
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThe present Multi-PI application will focus on defining specific factors and cells that may be targeted to treatIBD. We propose here an innovative central hypothesis that nanoparticle-mediated manipulation of TNF IL-12/23 and IL-22 limits established intestinal inflammation and promotes wound healing during IBD. We willexplore this hypothesis with state-of-the-art approaches employing siRNA nanoparticles that target keycytokinesin particular TNF and/or IL-12/23which are involved in the pathogenesis of IBD. Usingcomplementary models of acute and chronic intestinal inflammation we will further define which specificintestinal antigen-presenting cells should be targeted by nanoparticles and the beneficial effect of co-deliveringIL-22 in order to promote wound healing. These studies will help to define novel biological functions ofnanoparticle-mediated siRNA delivery during intestinal inflammation and have the potential to contributemeaningful new findings for the treatment of human IBD.

project_title: Virus-inspired nanoparticles for mucus penetrating gene delivery
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeHere this proposal is relevant to public health because it seeks to develop virus-inspired nanoparticles topenetrate mucus barriers in diseases (mainly cystic fibrosis (CF)) and effectively deliver gene editingcomponents as gene therapy to correct defective mutations in CF. From these insights we will able to developmore effective drug delivery systems able to penetrate through the complex impregnable mucus barrierpresent in CF. Importantly this technology can extend to drug delivery other diseases involving the mucosaincluding COPD and HIV. The proposed work is relevant to part of the NIH's mission to help furtherunderstanding biological systems and leveraging that knowledge to improve health and reduce illness.

project_title: IgE Suppressing Berberine Nanomedicine for Treatment of Food Allergies
public_health_relevance: NarrativeFood Allergy is a serious and sometimes fatal condition affecting nearly 15 million people in theUS for which there currently no cure. GNT LLC a biotech startup based in Elmsford NY hasdeveloped an innovative berberine-based nanoparticle NIT-X which in preclinical murine studiescauses profound selective and lasting abrogation of IgE and completely protects fromanaphylaxis. Unique ability of NIT-X to reduce IgE producing cells makes it a potentially breakthrough treatment for food allergy.

project_title: Improving Solubility Stability and Permeability of Natural Products Extracts
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeIsolation of natural products from various sources is still a challenging task and lead generation and validation ofnovel or known natural products is fraught with false negatives and false positives. Our goal is to improve theefficiency of natural drug discovery during the important extraction and isolation process through application ofnanoformulations to better solubilize and stabilize the chemical constituents for testing.

project_title: NELL-1's functional role in cartilage regeneration
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCECartilage injury and arthritic joint destruction is the leading cause of disability in the US afflicting over 46million Americans. To address the biomedical burden of such injuries we will investigate an innovativecartilage growing growth factor NELL-1 that we have optimally formulated to promote cartilage repair. Ifsuccessful this therapy will be highly translational for the early treatment of cartilage injuries to reduce thelikelihood of progressive cartilage injury and development of osteoarthritis.

project_title: Nanocarriers Designed to Deliver Nucleic Acids to Brain
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: The overall goal is to develop a novel drug delivery system to transport large therapeutic molecules (nucleic acids) from nose to brain in a non-invasive safe and effective manner. The research proposal consists of a series of experiments to test and refine the efficacy of this approach in silencing pathological genes expressed in transgenic mouse models. The ability to dose patients chronically and non-invasively by intra-nasal administration of nanocarriers of gene-silencing agents or other large therapeutic molecules will have a dramatic impact in the therapeutics of brain disorders.

project_title: Retinoids in Vision
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEThe retinoid (visual) cycle is a fundamental set of reactions necessary to regenerate the visualchromophore 11-cis-retinal and sustain vision. Genetic or environmental factors affectingchromophore production can lead to blindness. We aim to advance our understanding of theretinoid cycle and to develop strategies to stop progression of human retinal diseases usinganimal models related to this metabolic transformation. Pharmacologic interventions to savevision are now within reach due to a significantly improved understanding of these chemicaltransformations.

project_title: Iron oxide nanotheranostics for breast cancer
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal describes a nanotechnology-based approach that combines the gene and immune therapies totreat breast cancer by simultaneously inhibiting tumor growth and activating T cells for tumor tissuedestruction. Our approach involves the development of a non-viral nanovector that is able to overcome extra-and intra-cellular barriers and specifically deliver genes and induce gene expression only in breast cancercells. This synergetic strategy aims to address the limitations of current breast cancer therapies and ifsuccessful could substantially improve the treatment outcome and minimize deleterious side effects.

project_title: Novel Therapeutic Approaches For Treatment of Intestinal Inflammation
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThe present Multi-PI application will focus on defining specific factors and cells that may be targeted to treatIBD. We propose here an innovative central hypothesis that nanoparticle-mediated manipulation of TNF IL-12/23 and IL-22 limits established intestinal inflammation and promotes wound healing during IBD. We willexplore this hypothesis with state-of-the-art approaches employing siRNA nanoparticles that target keycytokinesin particular TNF and/or IL-12/23which are involved in the pathogenesis of IBD. Usingcomplementary models of acute and chronic intestinal inflammation we will further define which specificintestinal antigen-presenting cells should be targeted by nanoparticles and the beneficial effect of co-deliveringIL-22 in order to promote wound healing. These studies will help to define novel biological functions ofnanoparticle-mediated siRNA delivery during intestinal inflammation and have the potential to contributemeaningful new findings for the treatment of human IBD.

project_title: Virus-inspired nanoparticles for mucus penetrating gene delivery
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeHere this proposal is relevant to public health because it seeks to develop virus-inspired nanoparticles topenetrate mucus barriers in diseases (mainly cystic fibrosis (CF)) and effectively deliver gene editingcomponents as gene therapy to correct defective mutations in CF. From these insights we will able to developmore effective drug delivery systems able to penetrate through the complex impregnable mucus barrierpresent in CF. Importantly this technology can extend to drug delivery other diseases involving the mucosaincluding COPD and HIV. The proposed work is relevant to part of the NIH's mission to help furtherunderstanding biological systems and leveraging that knowledge to improve health and reduce illness.

project_title: Novel Therapeutic Approaches For Treatment of Intestinal Inflammation
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThe present Multi-PI application will focus on defining specific factors and cells that may be targeted to treatIBD. We propose here an innovative central hypothesis that nanoparticle-mediated manipulation of TNF IL-12/23 and IL-22 limits established intestinal inflammation and promotes wound healing during IBD. We willexplore this hypothesis with state-of-the-art approaches employing siRNA nanoparticles that target keycytokinesin particular TNF and/or IL-12/23which are involved in the pathogenesis of IBD. Usingcomplementary models of acute and chronic intestinal inflammation we will further define which specificintestinal antigen-presenting cells should be targeted by nanoparticles and the beneficial effect of co-deliveringIL-22 in order to promote wound healing. These studies will help to define novel biological functions ofnanoparticle-mediated siRNA delivery during intestinal inflammation and have the potential to contributemeaningful new findings for the treatment of human IBD.

project_title: Retinoids in Vision
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEThe retinoid (visual) cycle is a fundamental set of reactions necessary to regenerate the visualchromophore 11-cis-retinal and sustain vision. Genetic or environmental factors affectingchromophore production can lead to blindness. We aim to advance our understanding of theretinoid cycle and to develop strategies to stop progression of human retinal diseases usinganimal models related to this metabolic transformation. Pharmacologic interventions to savevision are now within reach due to a significantly improved understanding of these chemicaltransformations.

project_title: Chitosan Guided Bone Regeneration Membranes for Dual Local Delivery of Simvastatin and Raspberry Ketone
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativePatients with craniomaxillofacial injuries periodontal disease and or requiring dental implantprocedures often experience bone loss resulting in complications of masticatory functionspeech and altered aesthetics that affect health and the ability to function in society. Guidedbone regeneration membranes are widely used in these applications to augment bonehealing/regeneration by protecting bone grafted sites but current membranes are not ideal andsuffer high complication rates. The work described in this proposal is an innovative bioactivemembrane based on electrospun chitosan loaded with raspberry ketone a natural anti-inflammatory/pro-healing agent and simvastatin a member of the statin class ofpharmaceuticals that also stimulates bone healing. This novel membrane will then be able toprovide superior protection and augment healing by reducing inflammation and stimulatingbone formation that will significant improve healing in dental/craniomaxillofacial defectsespecially large traumatic defects.

project_title: Novel Therapeutic Approaches For Treatment of Intestinal Inflammation
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThe present Multi-PI application will focus on defining specific factors and cells that may be targeted to treatIBD. We propose here an innovative central hypothesis that nanoparticle-mediated manipulation of TNF IL-12/23 and IL-22 limits established intestinal inflammation and promotes wound healing during IBD. We willexplore this hypothesis with state-of-the-art approaches employing siRNA nanoparticles that target keycytokinesin particular TNF and/or IL-12/23which are involved in the pathogenesis of IBD. Usingcomplementary models of acute and chronic intestinal inflammation we will further define which specificintestinal antigen-presenting cells should be targeted by nanoparticles and the beneficial effect of co-deliveringIL-22 in order to promote wound healing. These studies will help to define novel biological functions ofnanoparticle-mediated siRNA delivery during intestinal inflammation and have the potential to contributemeaningful new findings for the treatment of human IBD.

project_title: Retinoids in Vision
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEThe retinoid (visual) cycle is a fundamental set of reactions necessary to regenerate the visualchromophore 11-cis-retinal and sustain vision. Genetic or environmental factors affectingchromophore production can lead to blindness. We aim to advance our understanding of theretinoid cycle and to develop strategies to stop progression of human retinal diseases usinganimal models related to this metabolic transformation. Pharmacologic interventions to savevision are now within reach due to a significantly improved understanding of these chemicaltransformations.

project_title: Overcoming Immunosenescence by Nanoparticle-Mediated Activation
public_health_relevance: Relevance to Healthcare Many vaccines are ineffective in elderly patients (>65 years) because of immunosenescence a gradual age-associated deterioration of the immune system. We have developed a particle- mediated adjuvant technology capable of delivering critical immunostimulatory cytokines directly to the draining lymph node the epicenter of the adaptive immune response and where antigen presentation to T cells occurs. By accelerating and maximizing the immune responses to previously non-responsive subpopulations this vaccine technology may significantly improve the healthcare of elderly patients.

project_title: Nanocarriers Designed to Deliver Nucleic Acids to Brain
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: The overall goal is to develop a novel drug delivery system to transport large therapeutic molecules (nucleic acids) from nose to brain in a non-invasive safe and effective manner. The research proposal consists of a series of experiments to test and refine the efficacy of this approach in silencing pathological genes expressed in transgenic mouse models. The ability to dose patients chronically and non-invasively by intra-nasal administration of nanocarriers of gene-silencing agents or other large therapeutic molecules will have a dramatic impact in the therapeutics of brain disorders.

project_title: A Novel Vaccine to Prevent Urinary Tract Infections
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Urinary tract infections are the most common bacterial infection in the United States disproportionately affecting women and exerting a negative impact on health care costs that is estimated at $2.4 billion annually. Although E. coli is known to be the primary cause of disease no vaccine is currently available. The project described in this proposal will develop an E. coli vaccine that combines two innovative technologies: isolation of a unique class of bacterial iron receptor proteins and a nanoparticle delivery system designed to produce a protective immune response in the urinary tract.

project_title: Multifunctional nanoparticles to improve treatment of human glioblastoma
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  We propose to develop a multifunctional nanoparticle that can deliver DNA repair inhibitors specifically to glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) cells to circumvent the therapy resistance and systemic toxicity. This novel strategy combines the advances in forefront research of GBM cancer biology with advanced nanotechnology in tumor imaging and therapeutics to address the current limitations in treatment of GBMs. Successful completion of the proposed work will produce a novel therapeutic agent ready to be brought to clinical trials in patients with GBMs.

project_title: Overcoming Immunosenescence by Nanoparticle-Mediated Activation
public_health_relevance: Relevance to Healthcare Many vaccines are ineffective in elderly patients (>65 years) because of immunosenescence a gradual age-associated deterioration of the immune system. We have developed a particle- mediated adjuvant technology capable of delivering critical immunostimulatory cytokines directly to the draining lymph node the epicenter of the adaptive immune response and where antigen presentation to T cells occurs. By accelerating and maximizing the immune responses to previously non-responsive subpopulations this vaccine technology may significantly improve the healthcare of elderly patients.

project_title: Magnetic Delivery of Therapeutic Nanoparticles to the Dental Pulp
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Inflammation of the dental pulp or pulpitis is a common painful global public health problem that affects quality of life of patients. In most instances pulpitis is a consequence of repeated insults from thermal stimuli and moderate or advanced tooth decay the most common chronic disease in the world. Available strategies for the treatment of pulpitis are aggressive costly painful and outdated. They are focused at excising the affected hard tissues (cavity preparation) and the placement of a filling or in advanced cases the radical amputation of the tooth pulp-the highly vascularized highly innervated inner layer of the tooth. In this application we describe an innovative technique to deliver medicationslocally to reduce tooth sensitivity and treat pulpitis. We exploit naturally occurring tubules withn the tooth in the dentin layer to magnetically control and deliver biocompatible nanoparticles carrying medications to the tooth pulp. This is a simple conservative and cost effective technique that may cause a paradigm shift in how dentists treat pulpal disease and may prove effective for other dental conditions that affect the teeth such as hypersensitivity and abscesses.

project_title: Engineered biomimetic materials for intestinal mucosal healing (Transfer)
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEDespite advances in available treatment achieving mucosal healing remains challenging for many patientssuffering from inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). In this proposal we will engineer a biomaterial-basedbiomimetic system that can selectively target the inflamed mucosa and locally release therapeutics to thedamaged epithelium. project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. project_title: Self-Assembling Camptothecin Nanofiber Hydrogels as Adjunct Therapy for Intraoperative Treatment of Malignant Glioma
public_health_relevance: Project narrative (relevance)One of the greatest hurdles to prolonging survival in patients with malignant brain tumors is the inevitable tumorrecurrence in the vicinity of the original location or the resection cavities despite multimodal treatment includingsurgery radiotherapy and chemotherapy. We aim to develop a camptothecin drug amphiphile (DA)nanofiber hydrogel platform technology as adjunct therapy for intraoperative treatment of malignant braincancer that can eradicate infiltrated glioma cells by maintaining a therapeutic concentration within the brain whileminimizing systemic drug exposure. This goal will be achieved by evaluating and optimizing our current hydrogel-forming DA technology with in vitro ex vivo and in vivo brain tumor models well-established in our laboratoryand the results of our study will validate a new local treatment strategy to combat currently incurable braincancers with significant survival benefits and good tolerance.

project_title: Drug delivery platform for ocular allergy therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEAmong the estimated 165 million worldwide contact lens wearers approximately one third of these suffer fromallergies but face a challenge as the application of eye drops is generally incompatible with lens wearing. Wepropose to develop a next generation 1-day drug delivery contact lens (DDCL) for olopatadine using boundarycharge modifiers to extend and control drug release the work will build on the Lynthera project team's previousaccomplishments in the field  enabling the use of the most efficacious allergy drug and the most popular contactlens material (silicone hydrogel). A daily disposable DDCL with prescription and loaded with olopatadine cansustainably deliver high-precision dosage for at least 12 hours to relieve allergy symptoms.

public_health_relevance: Project Narrative: Cochlear implants represent the most successful neuroprosthesis in clinical use providingdirect electrical stimulation to the auditory nerve to restore hearing to deaf patients. project_title: Dynamic Acellular Materials for Repairing Dense Connective Tissues
public_health_relevance: NarrativeMenisci of the knee cushion our joints during normal loading but are commonly injured. Unfortunately healing ofmenisci in adults is limited and current treatments do not restore function resulting in degeneration of theunderlying cartilage and progressive joint disease. To address this we engineer injectable biomaterials withtailored biofactor release to sequentially promote cell migration from surrounding tissue and direct matrixformation upon arrival at the injury site and test these in a clinically relevant large animal meniscus repair model.

project_title: Optimizing Therapeutic Revascularization by  Endothelial Cell_Transplantation
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEAs in the parent grant the supplement address a problem that is common to tissue engineering of allvascularized replacement organs namely how to incorporate a functional microvascular system for organperfusion. We will introduce two new innovations in this supplement: (1) we will use a new versatile degradablepolymer material for delivery of growth factors particularly VEGF in a time and spatially controlled fashionwithin our 3D bioprinted skin; and (2) we will use an innovative strategy for controlled slow extracellularrelease of mRNA polyplexes which will allow us to provide weeks long expression of mRNA creatingprolonged Bcl2 expression (which we have previously show to dramatically enhance vascular maturation)providing safer more transient expression than is possible with viral vectors.

project_title: Catheter-injectable engineered biomaterial for sustained Neuregulin-1 delivery to the myocardium
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeNeuregulin-1 (NRG1) is a promising recombinant protein therapy for myocardial infarction (MI) which like mostprotein therapies suffers from rapid loss of function during conventional intravenous administrationnecessitating high dosing repeated administration and/or long treatment periods. In this study we present thedevelopment and evaluation of a new engineered injectable liposome-crosslinked hydrogel for sustained NRG1delivery into infarcted myocardium. Success of this proposed study would enhance the outcomes of NRG1therapy for MI and provide a novel approach for sustained direct delivery of therapeutics into the heart.

project_title: A Relaxin-loaded Hydrogel for the Treatment of Hypertrophic Scars
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEHypertrophic scars arise in over 50% of all surgical procedures and 90% of burn injuries and are physicallyuncomfortable and painful as well as inflict psychological damage due to their negative cosmetic appearances.Current treatment options are limited in their scope and efficacy as they do not target the underlying cause ofscar development: a dysregulated wound healing response. Here the sustained and localized delivery of thenatural antifibrotic peptide hormone relaxin-2 via a hydrogel is described as a novel solution to treat both theunderlying etiology and symptomatic presentation of hypertrophic scars.

project_title: Engineering the open porous nanofibrous microsphere integrated fibrillar hydrogel for the co-delivery of antibacterial and angiogenic agents aimed at the rapid diabetic wound repair
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEPreventing infections vascularization cell migration and reintegration with surrounding tissues are vital factorsin diabetic wound healing. Here we propose to develop an extracellular matrix (ECM) mimicking porousnanofibrous microspheres (NMs) integrated with fibrillar hydrogel to co-deliver both antibacterial and angiogenicpeptides to the wound site to control infections and enhance vascularization during the diabetic wound healing.The novel open porous architecture of the NMs facilitates rapid cell migration and accelerates the healingprocess; thus our goal is to engineer an inexpensive effective and minimally invasive combinatorial treatmentstrategy to treat diabetic wounds.

project_title: Engineered biomaterials for promoting lymphatic regeneration in lymphedema
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThis proposed study aims to leverage biomaterial and drug delivery methodologies to achieve two primaryobjectives: firstly to augment lymphatic regeneration by integrating hydrogels that promote lymphatic growthwith vascularized lymph node transfer (VLNT) and secondly to encourage lymphatic vessel expansion at sitedistal to the original lymphatic injury using drug delivery to the lymphatics. This will be accomplished throughadvanced biomaterial implantation and targeted mRNA delivery to stimulate lymphatic growth factor production.The outcomes of this research endeavor are anticipated to pioneer a novel therapeutic approach for improvingthe efficacy of VLNT as a lymphedema treatment.

project_title: Drug eluting injectable biomaterials for next generation chemoembolization
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe 5-year survival rate of liver cancer patients is as low as 9.9% in the United States and 6.2% in Europe.Nearly 2 billion people worldwide infected with hepatic viral diseases are at risk for developing liver cancerand dying owing to limited therapeutic options. While historically systemic chemotherapy has been thecornerstone to cancer treatment inability to achieve uniform drug delivery to tumors collateral toxicity to non-cancerous liver and systemic side-effects have limited progress in the development of novel therapies for livercancer. We propose a transformative platform technology that uses a catheter based locoregionalapproach to deliver X-ray visible bioengineered biomaterial i.e. next generation TACE to induce a moreefficient ischemic cell death within the tumor microvasculature that can be coupled with chemo- andimmunotherapy. We believe that our approach will prevent progression of cancer providing time to bridgemore patients to the curative liver transplantation and extend lives.

project_title: SynerGel: A Novel Tumor Microenvironment-Modulating Hydrogel for Local Immunotherapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE.The overall 5-year survival rate for head and neck cancer has not improved much over the last few decades withboth conventional multimodal treatment and emerging immunotherapies known to have an adverse (andsometimes fatal) impact on quality of life. The goal of this proposal is to investigate the mechanisms driving theeffectiveness of a novel immunotherapy for head and neck cancer we call SynerGel and determine how it canbe used in combination with standard-of-care radiation therapy to reduce treatment toxicity. By doing so we aimto enhance our understanding of how both tumor and immune cells in the tumor environment can be manipulatedto produce a strong anti-tumor effect while reducing the side effects of treatment.

project_title: Conformal islet encapsulation for transplantation at vascularized sites to allow physiological insulin secretion
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIn the United States 1.84 million people have type 1 diabetes with 21% increase in diagnosis between 2001and 2009 and 5 million people expected to be diagnosed by 2050. Islet transplantation can cure type 1diabetes but it requires chronic systemic immunosuppression which limits the applicability of this procedureonly to the most severe cases of type 1 diabetes. Our innovative platform for islet microencapsulation throughconformal coating combined with our localized immunomodulatory approaches may eliminate the need forlifelong systemic immunosuppression and extend the applicability of islet transplantation to a larger number ofpatients with type 1 diabetes.

project_title: Nanobarrier contact lenses for mono and combination therapy for treating microbial keratitis
public_health_relevance: Microbial keratitis (MK) therapy by eye drops has many deficiencies including high frequency dosing and low dose deliveryto target tissues of about 1-5%. We propose to develop drug eluting contact lenses capable of delivering about 50% doseto target tissues allowing replacement of tedious drop therapy involving multiple drops each day with the use of a singlecontact lens for 3-days therapy followed by another lens for 3-days if needed. The proposed research aims to manufactureand characterize novel nanobarrier lenses and measure in vivo drug release and efficacy in an animal model.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat model we believe thatplacing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will still allow the brain toswell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Drug delivery platform for ocular allergy therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEAmong the estimated 165 million worldwide contact lens wearers approximately one third of these suffer fromallergies but face a challenge as the application of eye drops is generally incompatible with lens wearing. Wepropose to develop a next generation 1-day drug delivery contact lens (DDCL) for olopatadine using boundarycharge modifiers to extend and control drug release the work will build on the Lynthera project team's previousaccomplishments in the field  enabling the use of the most efficacious allergy drug and the most popular contactlens material (silicone hydrogel). A daily disposable DDCL with prescription and loaded with olopatadine cansustainably deliver high-precision dosage for at least 12 hours to relieve allergy symptoms.

project_title: Self-Assembling Camptothecin Nanofiber Hydrogels as Adjunct Therapy for Intraoperative Treatment of Malignant Glioma
public_health_relevance: Project narrative (relevance)One of the greatest hurdles to prolonging survival in patients with malignant brain tumors is the inevitable tumorrecurrence in the vicinity of the original location or the resection cavities despite multimodal treatment includingsurgery radiotherapy and chemotherapy. We aim to develop a camptothecin drug amphiphile (DA)nanofiber hydrogel platform technology as adjunct therapy for intraoperative treatment of malignant braincancer that can eradicate infiltrated glioma cells by maintaining a therapeutic concentration within the brain whileminimizing systemic drug exposure. This goal will be achieved by evaluating and optimizing our current hydrogel-forming DA technology with in vitro ex vivo and in vivo brain tumor models well-established in our laboratoryand the results of our study will validate a new local treatment strategy to combat currently incurable braincancers with significant survival benefits and good tolerance.

project_title: Treating Second-Degree Burn Wound Infections with a Quorum Sensing Inhibitor
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEBacterial infection of burn wounds is common and increases both the morbidity and mortality of burn injuries.Furthermore current burn dressings adhere to the wound surface and make each change of dressing excruci-atingly painful for the patient as well as cumbersome and time consuming for clinical personnel. This proposaldevelops a novel treatment that addresses difficult to treat infections (e.g. methicillin-resistant Staphylococcusaureus) wherein a new drug that prevents bacteria from communicating and coordinating attacks against thehost/patient is delivered via a novel wound dressing that alleviates patient pain during dressing changes.

project_title: Multiplexed Sensing and Control of Neuromodulators and Peptides in the Awake Brain
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal will develop new tools for real-time sensing of absolute concentrations of multipleneuromodulatory signals in the brain using advanced one-photon and two-photon imaging. A detailedunderstanding of the levels of many neuromodulators and how this neuromodulatory signature changes acrossbehavioral states is sorely lacking. Improved understanding and technology will allow testing of moresophisticated brain neuromodulation methods for improving cognitive and correction of chemical imbalances inthe brain across neurologic and psychiatric disorders.

project_title: Reduction of Intracochlear Trauma and Fibrosis Using Dual Network Zwitterionic Hydrogel Thin Films on Cochlear Implant Surfaces
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative: Cochlear implants represent the most successful neuroprosthesis in clinical use providingdirect electrical stimulation to the auditory nerve to restore hearing to deaf patients. However cochlear implantrecipients usually struggle to understand complex auditory signals such as speech in noise and music. Thesestudies seek to develop and understand novel thin film coatings applied to the implant to diminish trauma andscarring in the cochlea improve the interface of the implant with the cochlear tissues and enhance the abilityof cochlear implants to represent complex auditory signals.

project_title: PHMB-Impregnated Acellular Biologic Grafts for Treatment of Third-Degree Burns
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEBioAesthetics seeks to improve outcomes for patients with full-thickness burn wounds by creating a dermalacellular biologic graft with a biocompatible biodegradable tunable hydrogel-based drug delivery system thatprovides sustained local release of therapeutic agents. Our novel approach simultaneously provides a naturalbiologic scaffold for patient-mediated tissue regeneration while countering the onset of complications due toinfection during the course of wound healing. This Phase II project focuses on advancing our R&D efforts in aporcine burn model.

project_title: Engineered Neuroprotective Stem-Cell Exosomes for In Utero Spina Bifida Therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESpina bifida (SB) is the most common cause of lifelong childhood paralysis which results from the incompleteclosure of the neural tube and overlying spine during fetal development. Patients require life-long treatment asthey navigate lifelong paralysis cognitive disabilities musculoskeletal deformities and bowel and bladderdysfunction. We aim to develop a novel engineered hydrogel system for local and sustained neuroprotection tobe used in conjunction with a bone scaffold thus resulting in an optimal combination scaffold as a long-termtherapy for the in utero treatment of SB.

project_title: Engineered biomimetic materials for intestinal mucosal healing
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEDespite advances in available treatment achieving mucosal healing remains challenging for many patientssuffering from inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). In this proposal we will engineer a biomaterial-basedbiomimetic system that can selectively target the inflamed mucosa and locally release therapeutics to thedamaged epithelium. The proposed research will use innovative approaches that employ drug delivery not onlymimicking the mucus layer but also as a tool to interrogate epithelial repair at the site of mucosal inflammationto shed light on mechanisms of mucosal healing for IBD and many other mucosal barrier disorders.

project_title: Imaging and Targeting Lung Proteolysis in ARDS
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeAcute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is common complication of acute lung injury that is associatedwith a high mortality. We propose a multimodality imaging approach for evaluation of activation of anenzyme in the lung that has been shown to play an important role in disease progression. We will use thissame imaging approach to also assess a novel drug delivery system for local inhibition of this enzyme in thelung to prevent the progressive lung injury associated with ARDS.

project_title: A Relaxin-loaded Hydrogel for the Treatment of Hypertrophic Scars
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEHypertrophic scars arise in over 50% of all surgical procedures and 90% of burn injuries and are physicallyuncomfortable and painful as well as inflict psychological damage due to their negative cosmetic appearances.Current treatment options are limited in their scope and efficacy as they do not target the underlying cause ofscar development: a dysregulated wound healing response. Here the sustained and localized delivery of thenatural antifibrotic peptide hormone relaxin-2 via a hydrogel is described as a novel solution to treat both theunderlying etiology and symptomatic presentation of hypertrophic scars.

public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEPreventing infections vascularization cell migration and reintegration with surrounding tissues are vital factorsin diabetic wound healing. Here we propose to develop an extracellular matrix (ECM) mimicking porousnanofibrous microspheres (NMs) integrated with fibrillar hydrogel to co-deliver both antibacterial and angiogenicpeptides to the wound site to control infections and enhance vascularization during the diabetic wound healing.The novel open porous architecture of the NMs facilitates rapid cell migration and accelerates the healingprocess; thus our goal is to engineer an inexpensive effective and minimally invasive combinatorial treatmentstrategy to treat diabetic wounds.

With more than 100 millionAmericans suffering from musculoskeletal pain there is an urgent need to find safe effective andnon-addictive interventions to manage musculoskeletal pain. project_title: Drug eluting injectable biomaterials for next generation chemoembolization
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe 5-year survival rate of liver cancer patients is as low as 9.9% in the United States and 6.2% in Europe.Nearly 2 billion people worldwide infected with hepatic viral diseases are at risk for developing liver cancerand dying owing to limited therapeutic options. While historically systemic chemotherapy has been thecornerstone to cancer treatment inability to achieve uniform drug delivery to tumors collateral toxicity to non-cancerous liver and systemic side-effects have limited progress in the development of novel therapies for livercancer. We propose a transformative platform technology that uses a catheter based locoregionalapproach to deliver X-ray visible bioengineered biomaterial i.e. next generation TACE to induce a moreefficient ischemic cell death within the tumor microvasculature that can be coupled with chemo- andimmunotherapy. We believe that our approach will prevent progression of cancer providing time to bridgemore patients to the curative liver transplantation and extend lives.

project_title: Conformal islet encapsulation for transplantation at vascularized sites to allow physiological insulin secretion
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIn the United States 1.84 million people have type 1 diabetes with 21% increase in diagnosis between 2001and 2009 and 5 million people expected to be diagnosed by 2050. Islet transplantation can cure type 1diabetes but it requires chronic systemic immunosuppression which limits the applicability of this procedureonly to the most severe cases of type 1 diabetes. Our innovative platform for islet microencapsulation throughconformal coating combined with our localized immunomodulatory approaches may eliminate the need forlifelong systemic immunosuppression and extend the applicability of islet transplantation to a larger number ofpatients with type 1 diabetes.

project_title: Treatment and Mechanisms of Diabetic Fracture Healing
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeMolecular mechanisms for diabetes impaired fracture healing are largely unknown. Ciliopathieshave very recently been identified as key modulators of the relationship between diabetes andmetabolic physiology. We will pursue this relationship to better understand diabetic fracturehealing and as a mechanistic tool for the engineering design of novel therapeutic aids for thebenefit of diabetic patients suffering from bone fractures.

project_title: Immunomodulation of juvenile femoral head osteonecrosis
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeLegg-Calv-Perthes disease is one of the most serious conditions affecting the pediatric hip as 50% of patientswill develop debilitating osteoarthritis. The project will develop new necrotic bone reconditioning andimmunomodulatory treatments to target chronic inflammatory macrophage response that hinders boneregeneration in this hip disorder. Successful completion of this project will have immediate clinical impact byproviding a proof-of-concept for the minimally invasive yet potentially highly effective tissue engineeringstrategies to overcome current barriers to successful bone regeneration in Legg-Calv-Perthes disease.

project_title: Injectable Hydrogels to Deliver Gene Therapy for Myocardial Infarct
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Myocardial infarction (MI) is a leading cause of death in the US. While growth factor-encoding genes isa promising therapy for MI local delivery remains an unmet challenge due to poor injectability of gene carriersand low payload retention within the beating heart. We propose the design of an injectable biomaterial thataddresses these two specific challenges: first the gel undergoes dynamic crosslinking to facilitate minimallyinvasive catheter delivery; second the gel rapidly and robustly self-heals to localize therapeutic genes within thecontracting myocardium; thus enabling the clinical translation of gene-based MI therapies.

project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat model we believe thatplacing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will still allow the brain toswell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Nanobarrier contact lenses for mono and combination therapy for treating microbial keratitis
public_health_relevance: Microbial keratitis (MK) therapy by eye drops has many deficiencies including high frequency dosing and low dose deliveryto target tissues of about 1-5%. We propose to develop drug eluting contact lenses capable of delivering about 50% doseto target tissues allowing replacement of tedious drop therapy involving multiple drops each day with the use of a singlecontact lens for 3-days therapy followed by another lens for 3-days if needed. The proposed research aims to manufactureand characterize novel nanobarrier lenses and measure in vivo drug release and efficacy in an animal model.

project_title: Single Stage Surgical Intervention for Treatment of Severe Traumatic Brain Injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat model we believe thatplacing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will still allow the brain toswell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Long-Acting Short-Residing Nanochelators for Iron Overload Therapy
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative: High iron stores are toxic due to metal-induced oxidative stress that promotes multiple organdamage including cardiomyopathy liver cirrhosis hypertriglyceridemia diabetes and premature death. Usingan iron chelator-coated ultrasmall nanochelator that captures iron from the plasma and liver without distributingit into non-target tissues we aim to develop subcutaneous injectable hydrogel formulations with increasedexposure for 3 weeks. This strategy can decrease body iron burden and reduce the risk of iron-mediated organtoxicity without overt chelator-related side effects.

Intestinal disorders including inflammatory bowel disease(Crohn's disease ulcerative colitis) irritable bowel syndrome and colorectal cancer are significant public healthconcerns that affect millions of Americans each year and drug delivery for localized delivery can improve clinicaloutcomes when compared to systemic administration of drugs. The proposed study investigates an innovative approach using focused ultrasound responsivehydrogels as a spatiotemporally controlled drug delivery system for cytokines. project_title: Self-Assembling Camptothecin Nanofiber Hydrogels as Adjunct Therapy for Intraoperative Treatment of Malignant Glioma
public_health_relevance: Project narrative (relevance)One of the greatest hurdles to prolonging survival in patients with malignant brain tumors is the inevitable tumorrecurrence in the vicinity of the original location or the resection cavities despite multimodal treatment includingsurgery radiotherapy and chemotherapy. We aim to develop a camptothecin drug amphiphile (DA)nanofiber hydrogel platform technology as adjunct therapy for intraoperative treatment of malignant braincancer that can eradicate infiltrated glioma cells by maintaining a therapeutic concentration within the brain whileminimizing systemic drug exposure. This goal will be achieved by evaluating and optimizing our current hydrogel-forming DA technology with in vitro ex vivo and in vivo brain tumor models well-established in our laboratoryand the results of our study will validate a new local treatment strategy to combat currently incurable braincancers with significant survival benefits and good tolerance.

project_title: Engineering the open porous nanofibrous microsphere integrated fibrillar hydrogel for the co-delivery of antibacterial and angiogenic agents aimed at the rapid diabetic wound repair
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEPreventing infections vascularization cell migration and reintegration with surrounding tissues are vital factorsin diabetic wound healing. Here we propose to develop an extracellular matrix (ECM) mimicking porousnanofibrous microspheres (NMs) integrated with fibrillar hydrogel to co-deliver both antibacterial and angiogenicpeptides to the wound site to control infections and enhance vascularization during the diabetic wound healing.The novel open porous architecture of the NMs facilitates rapid cell migration and accelerates the healingprocess; thus our goal is to engineer an inexpensive effective and minimally invasive combinatorial treatmentstrategy to treat diabetic wounds.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Multiplexed Sensing and Control of Neuromodulators and Peptides in the Awake Brain
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThis proposal will develop new tools for real-time sensing of absolute concentrations of multipleneuromodulatory signals in the brain using advanced one-photon and two-photon imaging. A detailedunderstanding of the levels of many neuromodulators and how this neuromodulatory signature changes acrossbehavioral states is sorely lacking. Improved understanding and technology will allow testing of moresophisticated brain neuromodulation methods for improving cognitive and correction of chemical imbalances inthe brain across neurologic and psychiatric disorders.

public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVECells of the immune system are known to play a critical role in bone healing and regeneration. project_title: Engineered biomimetic materials for intestinal mucosal healing
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEDespite advances in available treatment achieving mucosal healing remains challenging for many patientssuffering from inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). In this proposal we will engineer a biomaterial-basedbiomimetic system that can selectively target the inflamed mucosa and locally release therapeutics to thedamaged epithelium. The proposed research will use innovative approaches that employ drug delivery not onlymimicking the mucus layer but also as a tool to interrogate epithelial repair at the site of mucosal inflammationto shed light on mechanisms of mucosal healing for IBD and many other mucosal barrier disorders.

project_title: Engineered Neuroprotective Stem-Cell Exosomes for In Utero Spina Bifida Therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESpina bifida (SB) is the most common cause of lifelong childhood paralysis which results from the incompleteclosure of the neural tube and overlying spine during fetal development. Patients require life-long treatment asthey navigate lifelong paralysis cognitive disabilities musculoskeletal deformities and bowel and bladderdysfunction. We aim to develop a novel engineered hydrogel system for local and sustained neuroprotection tobe used in conjunction with a bone scaffold thus resulting in an optimal combination scaffold as a long-termtherapy for the in utero treatment of SB.

project_title: Topical Drug Delivery System as an Anti-Inflammatory and Pro-Angiogenic Therapy for Diabetic Wound Healing
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEImpaired wound healing in ulcers that form on diabetic patients is a major clinical problem with prolongedhospitalizations and significant healthcare expenditures of $78 billion annually in the U.S. alone and with adesperate need for better treatment options. GelSana Therapeutics Inc. is addressing this need with itsproprietary wound healing hydrogels that have unique handling properties help reduce inflammation in thewound bed and are capable of providing sustained drug delivery to the wound bed over time to improve thediabetic healing response. Here the impact of a GelSana hydrogel-drug combination product on diabetic woundhealing will be determined which could be critical for helping to correct the diabetic impairment to wound healingand ultimately bring diabetic patients a new much-needed healing option.

project_title: Imaging and Targeting Lung Proteolysis in ARDS
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeAcute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is common complication of acute lung injury that is associatedwith a high mortality. We propose a multimodality imaging approach for evaluation of activation of anenzyme in the lung that has been shown to play an important role in disease progression. We will use thissame imaging approach to also assess a novel drug delivery system for local inhibition of this enzyme in thelung to prevent the progressive lung injury associated with ARDS.

project_title: Designing non-toxic hydrogels for trans-tympanic drug delivery and treatment of otitis media
public_health_relevance: This project aims to create a new non-toxic drug delivery hydrogel to directly and non-invasivelytreat acute otitis media (OM) a disease that affects 95% of US children. The proposal builds onprevious research showing successful eradication of OM using polymeric hydrogels by employingsolely chemistries approved by the FDA for topical oral and intravenous delivery which in theshort term can accelerate downstream clinical translation to improve child health. In the long termthis one-application direct delivery system has the potential to reduce treatment side effects curbantimicrobial resistance and improve treatment access and efficacy by removing barriers topatient compliance associated with oral therapy.

project_title: Reduction of Intracochlear Trauma and Fibrosis Using Dual Network Zwitterionic Hydrogel Thin Films on Cochlear Implant Surfaces
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative: Cochlear implants represent the most successful neuroprosthesis in clinical use providingdirect electrical stimulation to the auditory nerve to restore hearing to deaf patients. However cochlear implantrecipients usually struggle to understand complex auditory signals such as speech in noise and music. Thesestudies seek to develop and understand novel thin film coatings applied to the implant to diminish trauma andscarring in the cochlea improve the interface of the implant with the cochlear tissues and enhance the abilityof cochlear implants to represent complex auditory signals.

project_title: Novel injectable analgesic delivery system for musculoskeletal pain management
public_health_relevance: Prescription opioids have reached epidemic proportions in the United States and musculoskeletalpain is one of the most frequent reasons for opioid prescription. With more than 100 millionAmericans suffering from musculoskeletal pain there is an urgent need to find safe effective andnon-addictive interventions to manage musculoskeletal pain. The purpose of this application is todevelop a novel injectable anesthetic formulation that can provide pain relief for up to 7-14 dayswith minimum side effects.

project_title: Sustained Ocular Drug Delivery System for Anti-VEGF Agents
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeWidespread clinical use of monthly anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) therapy necessitates apractical and effective delivery method to the posterior segment of the eye. The overall goal of this proposal isto demonstrate the proposed drug delivery system can release bioactive anti-VEGF over a prolong period of 6months in a primate model and is effective as the conventional monthly treatment. The proposed drug deliverysystem will have a great impact on the current treatment of age-related macular degeneration and diabeticretinopathy.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury - DIVERSITY SUPPLEMENT
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVE [PARENT R01NS123051]When someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain canswell. Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and thepatient can experience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat modelwe believe that placing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will stillallow the brain to swell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Preventive and Therapeutic Effects of Bone Marrow Stem Cell-Derived Exosomes on Spine Dural Fibrosis
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Spinal dural fibrosis including epidural and peridural fibrosis is defined as non-physiologic scar formation andis the most common cause of failed back surgery syndrome which refers to a cluster of symptoms in spinal cordand nerve roots after spinal surgery. If not properly treated the dural fibrosis can generally worsen which mayrequire surgical treatments including percutaneous adhesiolysis and spinal endoscopy however success ofthese surgical interventions is only 30-35%. Thus there is a critical and urgent need to develop pharmacologicaltherapies of dural fibrosis and we propose mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosomes have the potential toprevent and treat spinal dural fibrosis driven by active myofibroblasts.

project_title: Drug eluting injectable biomaterials for next generation chemoembolization
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe 5-year survival rate of liver cancer patients is as low as 9.9% in the United States and 6.2% in Europe.Nearly 2 billion people worldwide infected with hepatic viral diseases are at risk for developing liver cancerand dying owing to limited therapeutic options. While historically systemic chemotherapy has been thecornerstone to cancer treatment inability to achieve uniform drug delivery to tumors collateral toxicity to non-cancerous liver and systemic side-effects have limited progress in the development of novel therapies for livercancer. We propose a transformative platform technology that uses a catheter based locoregionalapproach to deliver X-ray visible bioengineered biomaterial i.e. next generation TACE to induce a moreefficient ischemic cell death within the tumor microvasculature that can be coupled with chemo- andimmunotherapy. We believe that our approach will prevent progression of cancer providing time to bridgemore patients to the curative liver transplantation and extend lives.

project_title: Injectable Hydrogels to Deliver Gene Therapy for Myocardial Infarct
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Myocardial infarction (MI) is a leading cause of death in the US. While growth factor-encoding genes isa promising therapy for MI local delivery remains an unmet challenge due to poor injectability of gene carriersand low payload retention within the beating heart. We propose the design of an injectable biomaterial thataddresses these two specific challenges: first the gel undergoes dynamic crosslinking to facilitate minimallyinvasive catheter delivery; second the gel rapidly and robustly self-heals to localize therapeutic genes within thecontracting myocardium; thus enabling the clinical translation of gene-based MI therapies.

project_title: Immunomodulation of juvenile femoral head osteonecrosis
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeLegg-Calv-Perthes disease is one of the most serious conditions affecting the pediatric hip as 50% of patientswill develop debilitating osteoarthritis. The project will develop new necrotic bone reconditioning andimmunomodulatory treatments to target chronic inflammatory macrophage response that hinders boneregeneration in this hip disorder.
