project_title: Long-Acting Short-Residing Nanochelators for Iron Overload Therapy
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative: High iron stores are toxic due to metal-induced oxidative stress that promotes multiple organdamage including cardiomyopathy liver cirrhosis hypertriglyceridemia diabetes and premature death. Usingan iron chelator-coated ultrasmall nanochelator that captures iron from the plasma and liver without distributingit into non-target tissues we aim to develop subcutaneous injectable hydrogel formulations with increasedexposure for 3 weeks. This strategy can decrease body iron burden and reduce the risk of iron-mediated organtoxicity without overt chelator-related side effects.

project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat model we believe thatplacing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will still allow the brain toswell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Regenerative Lipid Mediators for the Management of Severe Burn Wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Current therapies are unable to overcome the healing defects in severe burns underscoring the urgentneed for more effective therapies. The successful completion of our proposed studies will provide: 1) a primaryhydrogel dressing that delivers regenerative lipid mediators (ReLiMs) and provides matrix and covering for theefficient healing of severe burn wounds; and 2) an innovative matrix that provides ReLiMs together with ReLiM-protected and guided uncultured adipose-tissue derived microvascular fragments for the more efficient healingof large deep burn wounds. These regenerative lipid mediator functionalized dressings with nonexistent orminimized graft-donor requirements are a promising adjuvant therapy to overcome the drawbacks of graftingmethods or skin substitutes currently used to treat severe burns.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Drug delivery platform for ocular allergy therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEAmong the estimated 165 million worldwide contact lens (CL) wearers many suffer from allergies but face achallenge as the application of eye drops is generally incompatible with lens wearing. We propose to develop anext-generation CL platform for allergy drugs delivery using boundary charge modifiers to extend and controldrug release the work will build on the Lynthera project team's previous accomplishments in the field enabling the use of both the most efficacious drugs and the best extended comfort lenses. A daily disposableCL with prescription and loaded with Olopatadine HCl can sustainably deliver high-precision dosage forextended hours to relieve allergy symptoms.

project_title: Dual-action drug coated balloon to mitigate neointimal hyperplasia and promote endothelialization.
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative It is estimated that 8.5 million people in the U.S. have peripheral artery disease (PAD) which can lead tofurther morbidities including life-style limiting claudication amputation and stroke. Up to 60% of patients whoreceive percutaneous transluminal angioplasty with uncoated balloons experience restenosis re-occlusion andrecurrence of symptoms. Current drug-coated balloons (DCB) are associated with concerns about increasedrisks inflammation and delayed endothelialization. Endomimetics LLC has developed a novel dual-actioncoating for DCBs that could reduce neointimal hyperplasia (NIH) and restenosis and enhance re-endothelialization and vascular healing.

project_title: Imaging and Targeting Lung Proteolysis in ARDS
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeAcute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is common complication of acute lung injury that is associatedwith a high mortality. We propose a multimodality imaging approach for evaluation of activation of anenzyme in the lung that has been shown to play an important role in disease progression. We will use thissame imaging approach to also assess a novel drug delivery system for local inhibition of this enzyme in thelung to prevent the progressive lung injury associated with ARDS.

project_title: Designing non-toxic hydrogels for trans-tympanic drug delivery and treatment of otitis media
public_health_relevance: This project aims to create a new non-toxic drug delivery hydrogel to directly and non-invasivelytreat acute otitis media (OM) a disease that affects 95% of US children. The proposal builds onprevious research showing successful eradication of OM using polymeric hydrogels by employingsolely chemistries approved by the FDA for topical oral and intravenous delivery which in theshort term can accelerate downstream clinical translation to improve child health. In the long termthis one-application direct delivery system has the potential to reduce treatment side effects curbantimicrobial resistance and improve treatment access and efficacy by removing barriers topatient compliance associated with oral therapy.

project_title: Engineered Neuroprotective Stem-Cell Exosomes for In Utero Spina Bifida Therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESpina bifida (SB) is the most common cause of lifelong childhood paralysis which results from the incompleteclosure of the neural tube and overlying spine during fetal development. Patients require life-long treatment asthey navigate lifelong paralysis cognitive disabilities musculoskeletal deformities and bowel and bladderdysfunction. We aim to develop a novel engineered hydrogel system for local and sustained neuroprotection tobe used in conjunction with a bone scaffold thus resulting in an optimal combination scaffold as a long-termtherapy for the in utero treatment of SB.

project_title: Design and characterization of biomimetic nanobiomaterials to elicit CD1-restricted T cell responses during sub-unit vaccination
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVELive attenuated bacterial vaccines elicit broad immune responses against both lipid and protein components yetcurrent subunit vaccine strategies do not sufficiently incorporate the lipid-specific mechanisms of immunity. project_title: Single stage surgical intervention for treatment of severe traumatic brain injury
public_health_relevance: NarrativeWhen someone suffers a severe brain injury a portion of the skull must be removed so that the brain can swell.Unfortunately a second surgery is required to replace that missing piece of the skull and the patient canexperience neurological problems up until the day that missing bone is replaced. In a rat model we believe thatplacing our new flexible material during the first surgery that can later turn into bone will still allow the brain toswell initially while avoiding the second surgery and the neurological problems.

project_title: Novel injectable analgesic delivery system for musculoskeletal pain management
public_health_relevance: Prescription opioids have reached epidemic proportions in the United States and musculoskeletalpain is one of the most frequent reasons for opioid prescription. With more than 100 millionAmericans suffering from musculoskeletal pain there is an urgent need to find safe effective andnon-addictive interventions to manage musculoskeletal pain. The purpose of this application is todevelop a novel injectable anesthetic formulation that can provide pain relief for up to 7-14 dayswith minimum side effects.

project_title: Immunomodulation of juvenile femoral head osteonecrosis
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeLegg-Calv-Perthes disease is one of the most serious conditions affecting the pediatric hip as 50% of patientswill develop debilitating osteoarthritis. The project will develop new necrotic bone reconditioning andimmunomodulatory treatments to target chronic inflammatory macrophage response that hinders boneregeneration in this hip disorder. Successful completion of this project will have immediate clinical impact byproviding a proof-of-concept for the minimally invasive yet potentially highly effective tissue engineeringstrategies to overcome current barriers to successful bone regeneration in Legg-Calv-Perthes disease.

project_title: Synthetic hydrogels to study formation and maintenance of intestinal crypts
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEWhen stem cells are isolated from the intestine and placed in culture they can self-organize to form complexthree-dimensional organoids that replicate many aspects of the functionality of the intestines. These miniatureintestinal organoids hold great promise as a tool to study development and disease screen the efficacy of drugdelivery systems or even produce transplantable tissue; however existing methods to grow intestinal organoidsrely almost exclusively on Matrigel scaffolds and Matrigels loosely-defined and variable composition makesclinical translation of intestinal organoids impossible. To address this issues we propose to develop a innovativehydrogel scaffolds for adult intestinal stem cells and use them to study and optimize their expansion colonyformation and differentiation to form organoids.

project_title: Drug eluting injectable biomaterials for next generation chemoembolization
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe 5-year survival rate of liver cancer patients is as low as 9.9% in the United States and 6.2% in Europe.Nearly 2 billion people worldwide infected with hepatic viral diseases are at risk for developing liver cancerand dying owing to limited therapeutic options. While historically systemic chemotherapy has been thecornerstone to cancer treatment inability to achieve uniform drug delivery to tumors collateral toxicity to non-cancerous liver and systemic side-effects have limited progress in the development of novel therapies for livercancer. We propose a transformative platform technology that uses a catheter based locoregionalapproach to deliver X-ray visible bioengineered biomaterial i.e. next generation TACE to induce a moreefficient ischemic cell death within the tumor microvasculature that can be coupled with chemo- andimmunotherapy. We believe that our approach will prevent progression of cancer providing time to bridgemore patients to the curative liver transplantation and extend lives.

project_title: Injectable Hydrogels to Deliver Gene Therapy for Myocardial Infarct
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Myocardial infarction (MI) is a leading cause of death in the US. While growth factor-encoding genes isa promising therapy for MI local delivery remains an unmet challenge due to poor injectability of gene carriersand low payload retention within the beating heart. We propose the design of an injectable biomaterial thataddresses these two specific challenges: first the gel undergoes dynamic crosslinking to facilitate minimallyinvasive catheter delivery; second the gel rapidly and robustly self-heals to localize therapeutic genes within thecontracting myocardium; thus enabling the clinical translation of gene-based MI therapies.

project_title: Sustained Triumvirate Delivery by Contact Lens for Post-Surgical Management
public_health_relevance: Post-surgical management after ophthalmic surgeries requires multiple eye drops of anti-inflammatory and antibiotics atdifferent frequencies and low bioavailability of 1-5%. We propose to develop drug eluting contact lenses capable ofdelivering about 50% dose to target tissues allowing replacement of tedious drop therapy involving tens of drops of threedifferent drugs with use of a contact lens for 7-days followed by another lens if needed. The proposed research aims tomanufacture and characterize the lenses and measure in vivo drug release in New Zealand White rabbit model.

public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThe proposed research is relevant to public health because it develops hydrogel-based materials for localizeddrug delivery to treat gastrointestinal diseases. Intestinal disorders including inflammatory bowel disease(Crohn's disease ulcerative colitis) irritable bowel syndrome and colorectal cancer are significant public healthconcerns that affect millions of Americans each year and drug delivery for localized delivery can improve clinicaloutcomes when compared to systemic administration of drugs. The proposed research will provide materials for3D printed drug delivery devices that can deliver a broad range of therapeutics for an indefinite period of time.

project_title: Regenerative Lipid Mediators for the Management of Severe Burn Wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Current therapies are unable to overcome the healing defects in severe burns underscoring the urgentneed for more effective therapies. The successful completion of our proposed studies will provide: 1) a primaryhydrogel dressing that delivers regenerative lipid mediators (ReLiMs) and provides matrix and covering for theefficient healing of severe burn wounds; and 2) an innovative matrix that provides ReLiMs together with ReLiM-protected and guided uncultured adipose-tissue derived microvascular fragments for the more efficient healingof large deep burn wounds. These regenerative lipid mediator functionalized dressings with nonexistent orminimized graft-donor requirements are a promising adjuvant therapy to overcome the drawbacks of graftingmethods or skin substitutes currently used to treat severe burns.

project_title: Preventive and Therapeutic Effects of Bone Marrow Stem Cell-Derived Exosomes on Spine Dural Fibrosis
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Spinal dural fibrosis including epidural and peridural fibrosis is defined as non-physiologic scar formation andis the most common cause of failed back surgery syndrome which refers to a cluster of symptoms in spinal cordand nerve roots after spinal surgery. If not properly treated the dural fibrosis can generally worsen which mayrequire surgical treatments including percutaneous adhesiolysis and spinal endoscopy however success ofthese surgical interventions is only 30-35%. Thus there is a critical and urgent need to develop pharmacologicaltherapies of dural fibrosis and we propose mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosomes have the potential toprevent and treat spinal dural fibrosis driven by active myofibroblasts.

project_title: Therapeutic bubble tea: Preventing the formation of uremic toxins with hydrogel immobilized microbes
public_health_relevance: Project narrativeIt is proposed that by lowering indole and p-cresol in the intestines the formation of indoxylsulfate and p-cresol sulfate in the liver can be reduced hence lowering two important protein-bound uremic retention solutes that otherwise cannot be efficiently removed with dialysis. Wewill utilize polymer and hydrogel engineering to tailor hydrogel beads that can be ingested asbubble teas and later be excreted intact from the GI tract. The beads will contain immobilizedbacteria and chemistry to degrade indole p-cresol or their amino acid precursors to non-toxicbeneficial or probiotic components and their performance will be tested by mathematicalmodelling and in bioreactor assays which simulate the trip through the gut.

project_title: Development of unimolecular nanoparticle-mediated periadventitial drug delivery system for sustained and targeted inhibition of intimal hyperplasia following open vascular reconstruction
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe development of recurrent disease following open vascular reconstruction is a major and expensive publichealth problem that affects hundreds of thousands of individuals in this country. Despite the availability ofmultiple techniques to prevent recurrent disease following intraluminal treatments of cardiovascular diseasethere is no effective strategy for patients that undergo open surgical reconstruction. We believe that this can besolved with the use of nanotechnology that can be directly applied to reconstructed vessels at the time of opensurgery. The combined expertise of a vascular surgeon a nanotechnologist and a vascular biologist creates aunique opportunity for creating a drug delivery nanoplatform to effectively prevent the recurrent disease thatfollows cardiovascular surgery.

project_title: Immunomodulation of juvenile femoral head osteonecrosis
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeLegg-Calv-Perthes disease is one of the most serious conditions affecting the pediatric hip as 50% of patientswill develop debilitating osteoarthritis. The project will develop new necrotic bone reconditioning andimmunomodulatory treatments to target chronic inflammatory macrophage response that hinders boneregeneration in this hip disorder. project_title: Designing non-toxic hydrogels for trans-tympanic drug delivery and treatment of otitis media
public_health_relevance: This project aims to create a new non-toxic drug delivery hydrogel to directly and non-invasivelytreat acute otitis media (OM) a disease that affects 95% of US children. The proposal builds onprevious research showing successful eradication of OM using polymeric hydrogels by employingsolely chemistries approved by the FDA for topical oral and intravenous delivery which in theshort term can accelerate downstream clinical translation to improve child health. In the long termthis one-application direct delivery system has the potential to reduce treatment side effects curbantimicrobial resistance and improve treatment access and efficacy by removing barriers topatient compliance associated with oral therapy.

project_title: Imaging and Targeting Lung Proteolysis in ARDS
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeAcute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) is common complication of acute lung injury that is associatedwith a high mortality. We propose a multimodality imaging approach for evaluation of activation of anenzyme in the lung that has been shown to play an important role in disease progression. We will use thissame imaging approach to also assess a novel drug delivery system for local inhibition of this enzyme in thelung to prevent the progressive lung injury associated with ARDS.

project_title: Engineered neuroprotective stem-cell exosomes for in utero spina bifida therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESpina bifida (SB) is the most common cause of lifelong childhood paralysis which results from the incompleteclosure of the neural tube and overlying spine during fetal development. Patients require life-long treatment asthey navigate lifelong paralysis cognitive disabilities musculoskeletal deformities and bowel and bladderdysfunction. We aim to develop a novel engineered hydrogel system for local and sustained neuroprotection tobe used in conjunction with a bone scaffold thus resulting in an optimal combination scaffold as a long-termtherapy for the in utero treatment of SB.

project_title: Drug eluting injectable biomaterials for next generation chemoembolization
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe 5-year survival rate of liver cancer patients is as low as 9.9% in the United States and 6.2% in Europe.Nearly 2 billion people worldwide infected with hepatic viral diseases are at risk for developing liver cancerand dying owing to limited therapeutic options. While historically systemic chemotherapy has been thecornerstone to cancer treatment inability to achieve uniform drug delivery to tumors collateral toxicity to non-cancerous liver and systemic side-effects have limited progress in the development of novel therapies for livercancer. We propose a transformative platform technology that uses a catheter based locoregionalapproach to deliver X-ray visible bioengineered biomaterial i.e. next generation TACE to induce a moreefficient ischemic cell death within the tumor microvasculature that can be coupled with chemo- andimmunotherapy. We believe that our approach will prevent progression of cancer providing time to bridgemore patients to the curative liver transplantation and extend lives.

project_title: Synthetic hydrogels to study formation and maintenance of intestinal crypts
public_health_relevance: NARRATIVEWhen stem cells are isolated from the intestine and placed in culture they can self-organize to form complexthree-dimensional organoids that replicate many aspects of the functionality of the intestines. These miniatureintestinal organoids hold great promise as a tool to study development and disease screen the efficacy of drugdelivery systems or even produce transplantable tissue; however existing methods to grow intestinal organoidsrely almost exclusively on Matrigel scaffolds and Matrigels loosely-defined and variable composition makesclinical translation of intestinal organoids impossible. To address this issues we propose to develop a innovativehydrogel scaffolds for adult intestinal stem cells and use them to study and optimize their expansion colonyformation and differentiation to form organoids.

project_title: Injectable Hydrogels to Deliver Gene Therapy for Myocardial Infarct
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Myocardial infarction (MI) is a leading cause of death in the US. While growth factor-encoding genes isa promising therapy for MI local delivery remains an unmet challenge due to poor injectability of gene carriersand low payload retention within the beating heart. We propose the design of an injectable biomaterial thataddresses these two specific challenges: first the gel undergoes dynamic crosslinking to facilitate minimallyinvasive catheter delivery; second the gel rapidly and robustly self-heals to localize therapeutic genes within thecontracting myocardium; thus enabling the clinical translation of gene-based MI therapies.

project_title: Regenerative Lipid Mediators for the Management of Severe Burn Wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Current therapies are unable to overcome the healing defects in severe burns underscoring the urgentneed for more effective therapies. The successful completion of our proposed studies will provide: 1) a primaryhydrogel dressing that delivers regenerative lipid mediators (ReLiMs) and provides matrix and covering for theefficient healing of severe burn wounds; and 2) an innovative matrix that provides ReLiMs together with ReLiM-protected and guided uncultured adipose-tissue derived microvascular fragments for the more efficient healingof large deep burn wounds. These regenerative lipid mediator functionalized dressings with nonexistent orminimized graft-donor requirements are a promising adjuvant therapy to overcome the drawbacks of graftingmethods or skin substitutes currently used to treat severe burns.

project_title: Engineered neuroprotective stem-cell exosomes for in utero spina bifida therapy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESpina bifida (SB) is the most common cause of lifelong childhood paralysis which results from the incompleteclosure of the neural tube and overlying spine during fetal development. Patients require life-long treatment asthey navigate lifelong paralysis cognitive disabilities musculoskeletal deformities and bowel and bladderdysfunction. We aim to develop a novel engineered hydrogel system for local and sustained neuroprotection tobe used in conjunction with a bone scaffold thus resulting in an optimal combination scaffold as a long-termtherapy for the in utero treatment of SB.

project_title: Sustained Ocular Drug Delivery System for Anti-VEGF Agents
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeWidespread clinical use of monthly anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) therapy necessitates apractical and effective delivery method to the posterior segment of the eye. The overall goal of this proposal isto demonstrate the proposed drug delivery system can release bioactive anti-VEGF over a prolong period of 6months in a primate model and is effective as the conventional monthly treatment. The proposed drug deliverysystem will have a great impact on the current treatment of age-related macular degeneration and diabeticretinopathy.

project_title: Surface Active Polyelectrolyte Nanomedicine for Treatment of Lung Infections in Cystic Fibrosis
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Patients with cystic fibrosis (CF) suffer persistent infections due to the buildup of mucus in their lungsand these are often complicated further by the formation of bacterial biofilms within CF sputum and by theemergence of multi-drug resistant organism strains. Our laboratory has developed graft polyelectrolytes withsmart surface-active chemistry that facilitates self-assembly with charged biomolecular cargoes and passageacross physiological barriers such as bacterial biofilms that limit drug distribution. In the proposed work wewill utilize these polyelectrolytes as regional drug carriers for tobramycin and colistin forming nanoparticlesthat can subsequently be aerosolized using a nebulizer to achieve high concentrations at the sites of infection.

project_title: Prevention of Children's Tympanostomy Tube Otorrhea
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeMyringotomy with tympanostomy tube insertion is the most common operation performed on children in theUnited States and is accompanied by frequent complications of otorrhea often containing active infection (otitismedia). An enhancement over the current treatment option would improve treatment efficacy increase patientcompliance and eliminate the temporary hearing loss. This CRP SBIR proposal involves the preparation ofmaterials for regulatory approval to initiate clinical trials of therapeutic treatments for otitis media associatedwith tympanostomy tubes in children.

public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVELive attenuated bacterial vaccines elicit broad immune responses against both lipid and protein components yetcurrent subunit vaccine strategies do not sufficiently incorporate the lipid-specific mechanisms of immunity. project_title: Novel injectable analgesic delivery system for musculoskeletal pain management
public_health_relevance: Prescription opioids have reached epidemic proportions in the United States and musculoskeletalpain is one of the most frequent reasons for opioid prescription. With more than 100 millionAmericans suffering from musculoskeletal pain there is an urgent need to find safe effective andnon-addictive interventions to manage musculoskeletal pain. The purpose of this application is todevelop a novel injectable anesthetic formulation that can provide pain relief for up to 7-14 dayswith minimum side effects.

project_title: Regenerative Lipid Mediators for the Management of Severe Burn Wounds
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Current therapies are unable to overcome the healing defects in severe burns underscoring the urgentneed for more effective therapies. The successful completion of our proposed studies will provide: 1) a primaryhydrogel dressing that delivers regenerative lipid mediators (ReLiMs) and provides matrix and covering for theefficient healing of severe burn wounds; and 2) an innovative matrix that provides ReLiMs together with ReLiM-protected and guided uncultured adipose-tissue derived microvascular fragments for the more efficient healingof large deep burn wounds. These regenerative lipid mediator functionalized dressings with nonexistent orminimized graft-donor requirements are a promising adjuvant therapy to overcome the drawbacks of graftingmethods or skin substitutes currently used to treat severe burns.

project_title: Injectable Hydrogels to Deliver Gene Therapy for Myocardial Infarct
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Myocardial infarction (MI) is a leading cause of death in the US. While growth factor-encoding genes isa promising therapy for MI local delivery remains an unmet challenge due to poor injectability of gene carriersand low payload retention within the beating heart. We propose the design of an injectable biomaterial thataddresses these two specific challenges: first the gel undergoes dynamic crosslinking to facilitate minimallyinvasive catheter delivery; second the gel rapidly and robustly self-heals to localize therapeutic genes within thecontracting myocardium; thus enabling the clinical translation of gene-based MI therapies.

project_title: A novel injectable piezoelectric hydrogel for osteoarthritis treatment
public_health_relevance: NarrativeOsteoarthritis is a major medical problem in the US and worldwide. Yet current medicinescannot completely cure the disease while surgical approach using replacement cartilage auto-and allo-grafts struggle with limitations in tissue supply infection immune-rejection invasiveimplantation and donor side morbidity. Here we propose a novel piezoelectric hydrogel whichcan be injected into an osteochondral defect to form an in situ cartilage graft and generateuseful electrical charge under ultrasound stimulation for enhancing cartilage growth and offeringan effective method to treat osteoarthritis.

project_title: Sustained Ocular Drug Delivery System for Anti-VEGF Agents
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeWidespread clinical use of monthly anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) therapy necessitates apractical and effective delivery method to the posterior segment of the eye. The overall goal of this proposal isto demonstrate the proposed drug delivery system can release bioactive anti-VEGF over a prolong period of 6months in a primate model and is effective as the conventional monthly treatment. The proposed drug deliverysystem will have a great impact on the current treatment of age-related macular degeneration and diabeticretinopathy.

project_title: Design and characterization of biomimetic nanobiomaterials to elicit CD1-restricted T cell responses during sub-unit vaccination
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVELive attenuated bacterial vaccines elicit broad immune responses against both lipid and protein components yetcurrent subunit vaccine strategies do not sufficiently incorporate the lipid-specific mechanisms of immunity. Wepropose the in vitro and in vivo optimization and characterization of a versatile nanobiomaterial-based vaccinedelivery system designed for simultaneous elicitation of both lipid- and peptide-specific immune responses.Engineered nanobiomaterials novel sustained release hydrogels and rationally selected antigen and adjuvantcombinations will be evaluated for toxicity and efficacy in unique humanized mouse models challenged withvirulent Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

project_title: Novel injectable analgesic delivery system for musculoskeletal pain management
public_health_relevance: Prescription opioids have reached epidemic proportions in the United States and musculoskeletalpain is one of the most frequent reasons for opioid prescription. With more than 100 millionAmericans suffering from musculoskeletal pain there is an urgent need to find safe effective andnon-addictive interventions to manage musculoskeletal pain. The purpose of this application is todevelop a novel injectable anesthetic formulation that can provide pain relief for up to 7-14 dayswith minimum side effects.

project_title: STINGel Immunotherapy for Treatment-Resistant Head and Neck Cancer
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeDespite advances in surgical techniques methods of radiation therapy and chemotherapeutic strategies theoverall 5-year survival rate for head and neck cancer has not improved much over the last few decades. Inaddition the potential morbidities associated with conventional multimodal treatment can have an adverseimpact on quality of life. The goal of this proposal is to investigate the mechanisms driving the surprisingeffectiveness of a novel immunotherapy for head and neck cancer we call STINGel. By doing so we aim toenhance our understanding of how both tumor and immune cells in the tumor environment can be manipulatedto produce a strong anti-tumor effect. This will provide insights into how next-generation biomaterial-basedimmunotherapy platforms such as STINGel can be further improved upon to broaden the scope of cancerpatients who can benefit from immunotherapy.

project_title: Development of a Polysaccharide-Based Patch for Use as a Therapeutic Lung Sealant
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:   Lung collapse can be caused by air or liquid leaking out of the lung into the pleural space (i.e. chest cavity). This results in significantly increased morbidity mortality and health care costs. The central goal of this application is to determine how the physical and mechanical properties of methacrylated polysaccharide hydrogels control drug release and to demonstrate in vivo pre-clinical efficacy for leak prevention and lung tissue wound healing.

project_title: Sustained Ocular Drug Delivery System for Anti-VEGF Agents
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeWidespread clinical use of monthly anti-vascular endothelial growth factor (anti-VEGF) therapy necessitates apractical and effective delivery method to the posterior segment of the eye. The overall goal of this proposal isto demonstrate the proposed drug delivery system can release bioactive anti-VEGF over a prolong period of 6months in a primate model and is effective as the conventional monthly treatment. project_title: Development of unimolecular nanoparticle-mediated periadventitial drug delivery system for sustained and targeted inhibition of intimal hyperplasia following open vascular reconstruction
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe development of recurrent disease following open vascular reconstruction is a major and expensive publichealth problem that affects hundreds of thousands of individuals in this country. Despite the availability ofmultiple techniques to prevent recurrent disease following intraluminal treatments of cardiovascular diseasethere is no effective strategy for patients that undergo open surgical reconstruction. We believe that this can besolved with the use of nanotechnology that can be directly applied to reconstructed vessels at the time of opensurgery. The combined expertise of a vascular surgeon a nanotechnologist and a vascular biologist creates aunique opportunity for creating a drug delivery nanoplatform to effectively prevent the recurrent disease thatfollows cardiovascular surgery.1

project_title: Vascular networks genetically engineered for protein drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Hemophilia A is an inherited bleeding disorder with no cure. Treatment involves repeated infusions of coagulation factor VIII throughout the life of the patient which creates tremendous discomfort and is extremely expensive. In response to these limitations we seek to develop a technology that uses patient's own cells to deliver FVIII into th bloodstream. To this aim we propose preclinical studies to establish feasibility of using drug-secreting vascular networks to treat the clotting deficiency of hemophilia A. We believe this novel approach has the potential to relieve patients from having to undergo frequent injections and to improve their quality of life.

project_title: Engineering a biopolymer hydrogel to inhibit osteogenesis: Implications for growth plate repair
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe growth plate cartilage found at the end of long bones in children that provides signals for the bones tolengthen as the child grows is fragile and prone to injury. Damage to the growth plate can cause angulardeformities in the growing bone or stop growth of the bone completely and there is no current treatment thatleads to repair of a damaged growth plate. Here we propose to develop biopolymer hydrogels with mechanicaland biochemical cues that can aid in healing injured growth plates.

project_title: A novel injectable piezoelectric hydrogel for osteoarthritis treatment
public_health_relevance: NarrativeOsteoarthritis is a major medical problem in the US and worldwide. Yet current medicinescannot completely cure the disease while surgical approach using replacement cartilage auto-and allo-grafts struggle with limitations in tissue supply infection immune-rejection invasiveimplantation and donor side morbidity. Here we propose a novel piezoelectric hydrogel whichcan be injected into an osteochondral defect to form an in situ cartilage graft and generateuseful electrical charge under ultrasound stimulation for enhancing cartilage growth and offeringan effective method to treat osteoarthritis.

project_title: Combined Hydrogel/Microparticle Eye Drops for Sustained Delivery of Glaucoma Medication
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness worldwide with this prevalence expected to increase substantially in coming years as it is more common in elderly adults.  The most common and preferred treatment to slow or stop the progression of glaucoma is the self-administration of topical eye drop medication as many as three times a day.  Eye drops are a highly inefficient treatment method due to limited retention and absorption of drug patient compliance issues due to the frequent dosing and side effects resulting from high levels of drug needed to achieve therapeutic effects.  Current efforts to develop new sustained release formulations that provide controlled delivery of glaucoma drugs for days or weeks (avoiding the need for topical drops) require invasive clinical procedures to insert or inject the drug delivery system and are associated with significant side effects themselves.  This project will investigate the potential of treating glaucoma with a noninvasive patient-administered eye drop that comfortably forms a gel in the area beneath the lower eyelid where it will provide up to one month of glaucoma medication.

project_title: Development of unimolecular nanoparticle-mediated periadventitial drug delivery system for sustained and targeted inhibition of intimal hyperplasia following open vascular reconstruction
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe development of recurrent disease following open vascular reconstruction is a major and expensive publichealth problem that affects hundreds of thousands of individuals in this country. Despite the availability ofmultiple techniques to prevent recurrent disease following intraluminal treatments of cardiovascular diseasethere is no effective strategy for patients that undergo open surgical reconstruction. We believe that this can besolved with the use of nanotechnology that can be directly applied to reconstructed vessels at the time of opensurgery. The combined expertise of a vascular surgeon a nanotechnologist and a vascular biologist creates aunique opportunity for creating a drug delivery nanoplatform to effectively prevent the recurrent disease thatfollows cardiovascular surgery.1

public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:   Lung collapse can be caused by air or liquid leaking out of the lung into the pleural space (i.e. chest cavity). This results in significantly increased morbidity mortality and health care costs. The central goal of this application is to determine how the physical and mechanical properties of methacrylated polysaccharide hydrogels control drug release and to demonstrate in vivo pre-clinical efficacy for leak prevention and lung tissue wound healing.

project_title: STINGel Immunotherapy for Treatment-Resistant Head and Neck Cancer
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeDespite advances in surgical techniques methods of radiation therapy and chemotherapeutic strategies theoverall 5-year survival rate for head and neck cancer has not improved much over the last few decades. Inaddition the potential morbidities associated with conventional multimodal treatment can have an adverseimpact on quality of life. The goal of this proposal is to investigate the mechanisms driving the surprisingeffectiveness of a novel immunotherapy for head and neck cancer we call STINGel. By doing so we aim toenhance our understanding of how both tumor and immune cells in the tumor environment can be manipulatedto produce a strong anti-tumor effect. This will provide insights into how next-generation biomaterial-basedimmunotherapy platforms such as STINGel can be further improved upon to broaden the scope of cancerpatients who can benefit from immunotherapy.

project_title: ANGIOGENIC TISSUE ENGINEERING TO LIMIT POST-INFARCTION VENTRICULAR REMODELING
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Myocardial ischemic injury in all of its forms from massive heart attack to mild chronic heart injury has become a tremendous world health problem. Current treatments reopen or bypass large blood vessels in the heart but fail to address a fundamental determinant of long-term benefit the availability of small microvessels to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the heart muscle---imagine driving in a country with abundant superhighways but no local roads to take you your final destination. We have developed techniques to stimulate the body's natural stem cells to build these microvessels into injured regions of the heart and propose in this application to study in further depth how these cells work how to increase the cell's efficiency and how to prepare this treatment for human clinical use.

project_title: Vascular networks genetically engineered for protein drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Hemophilia A is an inherited bleeding disorder with no cure. Treatment involves repeated infusions of coagulation factor VIII throughout the life of the patient which creates tremendous discomfort and is extremely expensive. In response to these limitations we seek to develop a technology that uses patient's own cells to deliver FVIII into th bloodstream. To this aim we propose preclinical studies to establish feasibility of using drug-secreting vascular networks to treat the clotting deficiency of hemophilia A. We believe this novel approach has the potential to relieve patients from having to undergo frequent injections and to improve their quality of life.

project_title: A facile approach for preparing dual-agonists with long lifetimes and balanced activities
public_health_relevance: We have developed a technology platform for half-life extension that is chemically controlled by a linker used to connect the drug to a carrier. We propose that by using the same linker to attach two different drugs to the same carrier we can confer the same half-life to both drugsand coordinate their in vivo concentrations. We will develop these concepts and apply them to produce a peptidic dual-agonist for treatment of diabetes and obesity.

project_title: Engineering a biopolymer hydrogel to inhibit osteogenesis: Implications for growth plate repair
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe growth plate cartilage found at the end of long bones in children that provides signals for the bones tolengthen as the child grows is fragile and prone to injury. Damage to the growth plate can cause angulardeformities in the growing bone or stop growth of the bone completely and there is no current treatment thatleads to repair of a damaged growth plate. Here we propose to develop biopolymer hydrogels with mechanicaland biochemical cues that can aid in healing injured growth plates.

project_title: Surgery to Prevent Post Infarction Ventricular Remodeling
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Left ventricular remodeling (enlargement of the heart) after myocardial infarction (heart attack) is the most common cause of heart failure in the United States and kills over 250000 Americans every year. The intent of this proposal is to engineer and test an optimized hyaluronic acid-based hydrogel material that can be delivered into the infarct region to mechanically stabilize the heart early after a myocardial infarction to prevent heart failure. The successful completion of the project would likely lead to a non-invasive means for reducing the incidence of heart failure.

project_title: Combined Hydrogel/Microparticle Eye Drops for Sustained Delivery of Glaucoma Medication
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness worldwide with this prevalence expected to increase substantially in coming years as it is more common in elderly adults.  The most common and preferred treatment to slow or stop the progression of glaucoma is the self-administration of topical eye drop medication as many as three times a day.  Eye drops are a highly inefficient treatment method due to limited retention and absorption of drug patient compliance issues due to the frequent dosing and side effects resulting from high levels of drug needed to achieve therapeutic effects.  Current efforts to develop new sustained release formulations that provide controlled delivery of glaucoma drugs for days or weeks (avoiding the need for topical drops) require invasive clinical procedures to insert or inject the drug delivery system and are associated with significant side effects themselves.  This project will investigate the potential of treating glaucoma with a noninvasive patient-administered eye drop that comfortably forms a gel in the area beneath the lower eyelid where it will provide up to one month of glaucoma medication.

project_title: Ophthalmic drug delivery using a multi-layer contact lens design that enables targeting constant-rate release and failure protection
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: The proposal aims to create a commercially viable contact lens for ocular drug delivery in patients with open angle glaucoma a disease that affects 2.8 million Americans and eventually leads to blindness. The current treatment standard of topical eye drops has notable limitations such as poor drug absorption (5-10%) and patient compliance (50% or less) while prior R&D attempts of contact lens drug delivery have failed to reach commercialization due to limitations in release duration release rate and biocompatibility. By solving these issues our proposed approach should supersede eye drops in drug absorption and compliance replacing them as the treatment method of choice in the $5 billion glaucoma drug market and creating a new platform to treat ophthalmic disease correct vision and perform diagnostic monitoring.

project_title: Prevention of Children's Tympanostomy Tube Otorrhea
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeMyringotomy with tympanostomy tube insertion is the most common operation performed on children in theUnited States and is accompanied by frequent complications of otorrhea often containing active infection (otitismedia). An enhancement over the current treatment option would improve treatment efficacy increase patientcompliance and eliminate the temporary hearing loss. This Phase II SBIR proposal involves the validation ofmanufacture and safety of therapeutic treatments for otitis media associated with tympanostomy tubes inchildren.

Current treatments reopen or bypass large blood vessels in the heart but fail to address a fundamental determinant of long-term benefit the availability of small microvessels to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the heart muscle---imagine driving in a country with abundant superhighways but no local roads to take you your final destination. We have developed techniques to stimulate the body's natural stem cells to build these microvessels into injured regions of the heart and propose in this application to study in further depth how these cells work how to increase the cell's efficiency and how to prepare this treatment for human clinical use.

project_title: Biomaterial Delivery System for Type 1 Diabetes Vaccine
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Type-1 diabetes is an autoimmune disease with enormous personal and economic impact in the US. Therapeutic vaccination approaches for type-1 diabetes hold promise to correct these autoimmune responses. The objective of this application is to engineer a biomaterials-based system with controlled release and targeting properties as an injectable vaccine to retrain the immune system to toward diabetes relevant self-antigen.

project_title: Vascular networks genetically engineered for protein drug delivery
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Hemophilia A is an inherited bleeding disorder with no cure. Treatment involves repeated infusions of coagulation factor VIII throughout the life of the patient which creates tremendous discomfort and is extremely expensive. In response to these limitations we seek to develop a technology that uses patient's own cells to deliver FVIII into th bloodstream. To this aim we propose preclinical studies to establish feasibility of using drug-secreting vascular networks to treat the clotting deficiency of hemophilia A. We believe this novel approach has the potential to relieve patients from having to undergo frequent injections and to improve their quality of life.

public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeStroke is the leading cause of adult disability in the United States. project_title: Advances in contact lens materials to extend wear time for a new standard in vision correction and future medical devices
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVESoft contact lenses are an $11 billion commercial market and worn by 39 million people in the U.S. (20% increasefrom 2000). In addition to limitations in design precision that affect visual clarity of the lens a major related healthproblem is lens biocompatibility which can lead to numerous patient complications and discomfort including notablyacanthamoeba keratitis ($175 million healthcare costs per year in the U.S.). We propose to solve this problem bydeveloping an advanced silicone hydrogel (SiHy) contact lens with novel tear exchange mechanism and better visualprecision fully compatible with the ocular environment. This new generation of materials will allow for extended weartimes beyond the current accepted modalities (1-2 week monthly) and create new opportunities for correctivefunctions (e.g. night vision enhancement myopia progression control) as well as a future platform for commerciallyviable ocular health monitoring and drug delivery lenses.

project_title: Polyphosphate Loaded Nanoparticles for Suppression of Gut Pathogen Collagenase Activity and Promotion of Post-Surgical Intestinal Healing
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Intestinal injury due to disease processes or to direct surgical manipulation can disrupt the normal microbiota and leave a large wound to heal in the presence of highly pathogenic bacteria. We have discovered that certain bacteria capable of producing high levels of the tissue-destroying enzyme collagenase can cause healing impairment leading to life-threatening problems such as obstruction and anastomotic leak. As an alternative to the use of antibiotics that eliminate normal microbiota which have been shown to enhance intestinal healing this proposal seeks to explore whether sustained release of polyphosphate will suppress collagenase production of problematic pathogens potentially leading to beneficial effects on intestinal healing and inflammation when locally delivered to sites of surgical injury.

project_title: Surgery to Prevent Post Infarction Ventricular Remodeling
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Left ventricular remodeling (enlargement of the heart) after myocardial infarction (heart attack) is the most common cause of heart failure in the United States and kills over 250000 Americans every year. The intent of this proposal is to engineer and test an optimized hyaluronic acid-based hydrogel material that can be delivered into the infarct region to mechanically stabilize the heart early after a myocardial infarction to prevent heart failure. The successful completion of the project would likely lead to a non-invasive means for reducing the incidence of heart failure.

project_title: Prevention of Children's Tympanostomy Tube Otorrhea
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeMyringotomy with tympanostomy tube insertion is the most common operation performed on children in theUnited States and is accompanied by frequent complications of otorrhea often containing active infection (otitismedia). An enhancement over the current treatment option would improve treatment efficacy increase patientcompliance and eliminate the temporary hearing loss. This Phase II SBIR proposal involves the validation ofmanufacture and safety of therapeutic treatments for otitis media associated with tympanostomy tubes inchildren.

project_title: Combined Hydrogel/Microparticle Eye Drops for Sustained Delivery of Glaucoma Medication
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness worldwide with this prevalence expected to increase substantially in coming years as it is more common in elderly adults.  The most common and preferred treatment to slow or stop the progression of glaucoma is the self-administration of topical eye drop medication as many as three times a day.  Eye drops are a highly inefficient treatment method due to limited retention and absorption of drug patient compliance issues due to the frequent dosing and side effects resulting from high levels of drug needed to achieve therapeutic effects.  Current efforts to develop new sustained release formulations that provide controlled delivery of glaucoma drugs for days or weeks (avoiding the need for topical drops) require invasive clinical procedures to insert or inject the drug delivery system and are associated with significant side effects themselves.  This project will investigate the potential of treating glaucoma with a noninvasive patient-administered eye drop that comfortably forms a gel in the area beneath the lower eyelid where it will provide up to one month of glaucoma medication.

project_title: Development of unimolecular nanoparticle-mediated periadventitial drug delivery system for sustained and targeted inhibition of intimal hyperplasia following open vascular reconstruction
public_health_relevance: NarrativeThe development of recurrent disease following open vascular reconstruction is a major and expensive publichealth problem that affects hundreds of thousands of individuals in this country. Despite the availability ofmultiple techniques to prevent recurrent disease following intraluminal treatments of cardiovascular diseasethere is no effective strategy for patients that undergo open surgical reconstruction. We believe that this can besolved with the use of nanotechnology that can be directly applied to reconstructed vessels at the time of opensurgery. The combined expertise of a vascular surgeon a nanotechnologist and a vascular biologist creates aunique opportunity for creating a drug delivery nanoplatform to effectively prevent the recurrent disease thatfollows cardiovascular surgery.1

project_title: Ophthalmic drug delivery using a multi-layer contact lens design that enables targeting constant-rate release and failure protection
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: The proposal aims to create a commercially viable contact lens for ocular drug delivery in patients with open angle glaucoma a disease that affects 2.8 million Americans and eventually leads to blindness. The current treatment standard of topical eye drops has notable limitations such as poor drug absorption (5-10%) and patient compliance (50% or less) while prior R&D attempts of contact lens drug delivery have failed to reach commercialization due to limitations in release duration release rate and biocompatibility. By solving these issues our proposed approach should supersede eye drops in drug absorption and compliance replacing them as the treatment method of choice in the $5 billion glaucoma drug market and creating a new platform to treat ophthalmic disease correct vision and perform diagnostic monitoring.

project_title: Mechanistic analysis of transport through the mucus barrier
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  The surface properties of pathogenic viruses or drug delivery vehicles determine how fast they can penetrate the mucus barrier yet the critical parameters that govern their transport are poorly understood and not possible to predict. The aims of this project are to predict whether a variety of synthetic or natural particles having complex physicalchemical and biological properties can pass through the mucus barrier in the body. This project will reveal molecular fingerprints that are diagnostic for fast and slow passage through mucus and will thereby significantly impact societal health by providing new strategies for preventing prevalent infectious diseases and for facilitating drug delivery.

project_title: ANGIOGENIC TISSUE ENGINEERING TO LIMIT POST-INFARCTION VENTRICULAR REMODELING
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Myocardial ischemic injury in all of its forms from massive heart attack to mild chronic heart injury has become a tremendous world health problem. Current treatments reopen or bypass large blood vessels in the heart but fail to address a fundamental determinant of long-term benefit the availability of small microvessels to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the heart muscle---imagine driving in a country with abundant superhighways but no local roads to take you your final destination. We have developed techniques to stimulate the body's natural stem cells to build these microvessels into injured regions of the heart and propose in this application to study in further depth how these cells work how to increase the cell's efficiency and how to prepare this treatment for human clinical use.

project_title: Polyphosphate Loaded Nanoparticles for Suppression of Gut Pathogen Collagenase Activity and Promotion of Post-Surgical Intestinal Healing
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Intestinal injury due to disease processes or to direct surgical manipulation can disrupt the normal microbiota and leave a large wound to heal in the presence of highly pathogenic bacteria. We have discovered that certain bacteria capable of producing high levels of the tissue-destroying enzyme collagenase can cause healing impairment leading to life-threatening problems such as obstruction and anastomotic leak. As an alternative to the use of antibiotics that eliminate normal microbiota which have been shown to enhance intestinal healing this proposal seeks to explore whether sustained release of polyphosphate will suppress collagenase production of problematic pathogens potentially leading to beneficial effects on intestinal healing and inflammation when locally delivered to sites of surgical injury.

project_title: Targeted Microcarrier Design and Optimization
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Drugs can be packaged as deliverable cargo into nanocarrier particles designed to target diseased tissue directly via bloodstream delivery.  We are developing  a computational model  to optimize vascular drug delivery including dispersal of the delivered cargo within target cells. Our model will be validated through synergistic animal and cell culture experiments of nanocarrier binding mechanics and cargo offloading efficiency. This work will provide computationally quantitative and predictive methods to optimize targeted nanocarrier design for drug therapies used to treat many diseases.

public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Left ventricular remodeling (enlargement of the heart) after myocardial infarction (heart attack) is the most common cause of heart failure in the United States and kills over 250000 Americans every year. The intent of this proposal is to engineer and test an optimized hyaluronic acid-based hydrogel material that can be delivered into the infarct region to mechanically stabilize the heart early after a myocardial infarction to prevent heart failure. The successful completion of the project would likely lead to a non-invasive means for reducing the incidence of heart failure.

project_title: Combined Hydrogel/Microparticle Eye Drops for Sustained Delivery of Glaucoma Medication
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness worldwide with this prevalence expected to increase substantially in coming years as it is more common in elderly adults.  The most common and preferred treatment to slow or stop the progression of glaucoma is the self-administration of topical eye drop medication as many as three times a day.  Eye drops are a highly inefficient treatment method due to limited retention and absorption of drug patient compliance issues due to the frequent dosing and side effects resulting from high levels of drug needed to achieve therapeutic effects.  Current efforts to develop new sustained release formulations that provide controlled delivery of glaucoma drugs for days or weeks (avoiding the need for topical drops) require invasive clinical procedures to insert or inject the drug delivery system and are associated with significant side effects themselves.  This project will investigate the potential of treating glaucoma with a noninvasive patient-administered eye drop that comfortably forms a gel in the area beneath the lower eyelid where it will provide up to one month of glaucoma medication.

public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Hemophilia A is an inherited bleeding disorder with no cure. Treatment involves repeated infusions of coagulation factor VIII throughout the life of the patient which creates tremendous discomfort and is extremely expensive. In response to these limitations we seek to develop a technology that uses patient's own cells to deliver FVIII into th bloodstream. To this aim we propose preclinical studies to establish feasibility of using drug-secreting vascular networks to treat the clotting deficiency of hemophilia A. project_title: Growth factor delivery using light-sensitive fibrin to treat chronic wounds
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  We have developed a novel strategy for the on-demand release of biologically active molecules via visible light actuation by incorporating non-toxic biocompatible chromophores - either riboflavin or methylene blue - to generate heat and act upon a photothermally responsive carrier. This proposal aims to optimize our system for treatment of chronic skin wounds. Fibrin scaffolds which are an ideal choice for treating chronic wounds but are not photothermally responsive are functionalized with heat-liable DNA tethers for the on-demand delivery of platelet derived growth factor (PDGF) vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF) or transforming growth factor-beta (TGF).

project_title: Mechanistic analysis of transport through the mucus barrier
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  The surface properties of pathogenic viruses or drug delivery vehicles determine how fast they can penetrate the mucus barrier yet the critical parameters that govern their transport are poorly understood and not possible to predict. The aims of this project are to predict whether a variety of synthetic or natural particles having complex physicalchemical and biological properties can pass through the mucus barrier in the body. This project will reveal molecular fingerprints that are diagnostic for fast and slow passage through mucus and will thereby significantly impact societal health by providing new strategies for preventing prevalent infectious diseases and for facilitating drug delivery.

public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Left ventricular remodeling (enlargement of the heart) after myocardial infarction (heart attack) is the most common cause of heart failure in the United States and kills over 250000 Americans every year. The intent of this proposal is to engineer and test an optimized hyaluronic acid-based hydrogel material that can be delivered into the infarct region to mechanically stabilize the heart early after a myocardial infarction to prevent heart failure. The successful completion of the project would likely lead to a non-invasive means for reducing the incidence of heart failure.

project_title: Biomaterial Delivery System for Type 1 Diabetes Vaccine
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Type-1 diabetes is an autoimmune disease with enormous personal and economic impact in the US. Therapeutic vaccination approaches for type-1 diabetes hold promise to correct these autoimmune responses. The objective of this application is to engineer a biomaterials-based system with controlled release and targeting properties as an injectable vaccine to retrain the immune system to toward diabetes relevant self-antigen.

project_title: Microparticles for Directing Immune Cell Trafficking
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: We will develop a new micro-and nanoparticle technology based on a hydrogel incorporating affinity baits capable of controlled binding and release of chemokines providing a predictable manipulation of the immune cell chemotaxis. The biological activity of the different chemokine-loaded particles will be investigated in a mouse model challenged with B. anthracis spores by assessing increased trafficking of neutrophils to lymph nodes. A global proteomic analysis of lymph from the infected lymph nodes will be conducted to gain new insights into host responses to infection and their modulation by administered chemokines.

project_title: ANGIOGENIC TISSUE ENGINEERING TO LIMIT POST-INFARCTION VENTRICULAR REMODELING
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Myocardial ischemic injury in all of its forms from massive heart attack to mild chronic heart injury has become a tremendous world health problem. Current treatments reopen or bypass large blood vessels in the heart but fail to address a fundamental determinant of long-term benefit the availability of small microvessels to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the heart muscle---imagine driving in a country with abundant superhighways but no local roads to take you your final destination. We have developed techniques to stimulate the body's natural stem cells to build these microvessels into injured regions of the heart and propose in this application to study in further depth how these cells work how to increase the cell's efficiency and how to prepare this treatment for human clinical use.

project_title: Targeted Microcarrier Design and Optimization
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Drugs can be packaged as deliverable cargo into nanocarrier particles designed to target diseased tissue directly via bloodstream delivery.  We are developing  a computational model  to optimize vascular drug delivery including dispersal of the delivered cargo within target cells. Our model will be validated through synergistic animal and cell culture experiments of nanocarrier binding mechanics and cargo offloading efficiency. This work will provide computationally quantitative and predictive methods to optimize targeted nanocarrier design for drug therapies used to treat many diseases.

project_title: Phase-transition Hydrogel Microneedle
public_health_relevance:
PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE: Despite decades of research efforts to develop an alternative delivery solution insulin an essential medicine for managing diabetes is typically administered to patients by frequent injections. Our newly developed insulin skin patch delivery system called phase-transition microneedle (PTM) patch circumvents any pain sensation or skin damage by utilizing an array of polymer microneedles pre-loaded with insulin that absorb fluid from the skin to swell and accurately and efficiently deliver insulin therapy transdermally. The present study is
aimed to develop and characterize this preliminary invention into a medicinal patch to replace daily insulin injections for controlling the basal blood sugar of diabetic patients.

project_title: Development of a Biomimetic Vitreous Substitute
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  The proposed research is relevant to public health because a biomimetic vitreous gel substitute would yield a much-needed alternative to non-physiological vitreous substitutes such as perfluorocarbons and silicone oils thus dramatically decreasing patient burden.  The vitreous gel plays a central role in the growth and overall health of the eye. To avoid the long-term deleterious effects of current materials it is critical that vitreous substitutes mimic the natural vitreous.  This project is in line with the underlying mission of theNIH since we seek to understand how the vitreous has been engineered in nature and will apply that understanding to the prevention or decrease of vision loss.

project_title: Combined Hydrogel/Microparticle Eye Drops for Sustained Delivery of Glaucoma Medication
public_health_relevance: PUBLIC HEALTH RELEVANCE:  Glaucoma is the second leading cause of blindness worldwide with this prevalence expected to increase substantially in coming years as it is more common in elderly adults.  The most common and preferred treatment to slow or stop the progression of glaucoma is the self-administration of topical eye drop medication as many as three times a day.  Eye drops are a highly inefficient treatment method due to limited retention and absorption of drug patient compliance issues due to the frequent dosing and side effects resulting from high levels of drug needed to achieve therapeutic effects.  Current efforts to develop new sustained release formulations that provide controlled delivery of glaucoma drugs for days or weeks (avoiding the need for topical drops) require invasive clinical procedures to insert or inject the drug delivery system and are associated with significant side effects themselves.  This project will investigate the potential of treating glaucoma with a noninvasive patient-administered eye drop that comfortably forms a gel in the area beneath the lower eyelid where it will provide up to one month of glaucoma medication.

project_title: Hydrogel template method for protein microencapsulation
public_health_relevance: Protein drugs have been essential in treating various diseases and yet the long-term delivery ranging from 1 month to 3 months has not been easy. The goal of this project is to use the newly developed hydrogel template-based nanofabrication methodology to prepare microparticles for more efficient long-term delivery of genetically engineered protein drugs.

project_title: Biodegradable Thermoresponsive Hydrogels to Treat Ischemic Cardiomyopathy
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeIt is estimated that 785000 Americans will have a heart attack annually and many of those who survive willultimately face deteriorating function of their heart leading to later death. This research seeks to develop anapproach where a gel-like material is injected into the heart after a heart attack to prevent the deteriorationin heart function. This gel is being designed to protect the heart mechanically from further damage after theheart attack and to help stimulate the heart to heal itself.

project_title: Therapeutic Application of Painless Nerve Growth Factor to Accelerate Endochondral Fracture Repair
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVECurrently there are no well-adopted FDA approved drug therapies available to accelerate fracture healing andpatients with poorly healing fractures typically require surgical intervention. The long-term goal of this project isto develop and validate an injectable biodegradable nanowire delivery platform for local and sustained releaseof nerve growth factor (NGF) to accelerate fracture healing. This grant proposal focuses on optimizing the timingdose and delivery platform for a novel painless isoform of NGF while also for the first time aiming to rigorouslydetermine the mechanism of action for NGF in bone regeneration.

project_title: Sustained release of fenofibrate for the treatment of diabetic retinopathy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVEThis training supplement will provide Julio Ocana-Ortiz BS a PhD student deriving froma disadvantaged background an opportunity to learn cutting edge technique that will aid hisdevelopment as an independent NEI-funded researcher. Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the mostcommon cause of severe vision loss in the working-age population in the world and fenofibratea PPAR agonist has shown the therapeutic effects on DR. project_title: Multifunctional Biodegradable Zwitterionic Polymer-Drug Conjugates for Multidrug Co-Delivery
public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeThe applicability of polymer-based drug delivery systems (DDSs) has been significantly restricted by criticaltechnological challenges such as the undesired immunogenicity of polyethylene glycol (PEG) broadly used inDDSs the lack of PEG alternatives with optimal comprehensive properties the scarcity of clinically valid DDSsfor combination therapy and the deficient understanding on topological effects of polymer scaffolds. To addressthese challenges we aim to develop multifunctional biodegradable zwitterionic polymer-drug conjugates(ZPDCs) as novel PEG-free multidrug co-delivery systems with favorable biomedically relevant properties. Alibrary of ZPDCs with different main-chain topology and other structural features will be prepared and theirstructure-dependent biointerface properties will be systematically investigated through biochemical cellular andin vivo studies.

project_title: Sustained release of fenofibrate for the treatment of diabetic retinopathy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the most common cause of severe vision loss in theworking-age population in the world and the standard drug treatment is the intravitreal (IVT)injection of anti-VEGF agents that must be frequently administered and are not adequatelyresponded by 40% DR patients. Oral administration of fenofibrate a PPAR agonist has shownrobust therapeutic effects on DR in type 2 diabetic patients; however oral fenofibrate has poorretinal absorption and IVT injection of fenofibrate is short-lived requiring frequent IVT injections.Here we propose a new method to provide sustained delivery of fenofibrate to effectively andsafely treat DR with 2 or less IVT injections/year significantly reducing the treatment burden ofdiabetic patients.

project_title: Minibeam Radiation Therapy Enhanced Delivery of Nanoparticle Anticancer Agents to Pancreatic Cancer Tumors
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE:The promise of nanoparticle (NP) anticancer drugs remain largely unfulfilled due to a low tumor delivery in whichonly 5-10% of these agents in the plasma are actually distributed into solid tumors. Thus there is a strong needto discover methods that can capitalize on the promise of NP anticancer drugs by significantly and safelyenhancing tumor delivery especially in pancreatic cancer (PaCa) that has major inherent barriers to tumordelivery that are associated with high resistance and low response. Our proposal will evaluate if inductionminibeam radiation therapy (MRT) in contrast to conventional broad beam radiation therapy (BRT) increasestumor perfusion and the tumor delivery and efficacy of NPs in PaCa tumor models as the induction MRT + NPregimen would be an ideal fit for the pre-surgical neoadjuvant treatment of PaCa.

public_health_relevance: Project NarrativeAntiviral therapeutics approved by the FDA or currently in clinical trials for treatment of chronic hepatitis B canefficiently suppress the replication of hepatitis B virus (HBV) reduce viral load and improve liver pathologybut fail to cure the chronic infection of HBV. This is because those drugs cannot eliminate the most stableHBV replication intermediate covalently closed circular DNA (cccDNA) in the nuclei of hepatocytes and viralreplication rebounds after discontinuation of treatment. This project addresses the pressing need for the cureof chronic hepatitis B by elimination and/or permanent inactivation of cccDNA with our proprietary chemicallyligated guide RNA (lgRNA)-based CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing technology.

project_title: Zwitterionic polyethylene glycol for therapeutic delivery
public_health_relevance: Project Narrative Biologic drugs and nanomedicines with conjugated polyethylene glycol (PEG) show improvedpharmacokinetics. However the application of PEGylated therapeutics is limited by the presence of anti-PEGantibodies which not only accelerate the clearance of PEGylated therapeutics and attenuate their efficacies butmay also cause severe side effects. We propose to develop zwitterionic PEG as a replacement of PEG intherapeutic delivery for minimized side effects and consistent efficacy.

project_title: Improving the Immune Response to Nanoparticle-Based SARS-CoV-2 Vaccines
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Although vaccines have been effective at protecting against infectious diseases that poseserious threats to human health they can also be limited when variants of the pathogen emergeyielding lower neutralization efficacy and requiring booster immunizations  consequences whichare observed in the current COVID-19 global pandemic. This work aims to redesign conventionalvaccines using hydrogels to improve their durability and breadth of immune response. Ultimatelysuccessful implementation of this approach could provide a more effective immune response tothe SARS-CoV-2 virus and its emerging variants and provide a general vaccine design strategythat could have broader applicability toward other infectious pathogens.

project_title: Sustained release of fenofibrate for the treatment of diabetic retinopathy
public_health_relevance: PROJECT NARRATIVE Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is the most common cause of severe vision loss in theworking-age population in the world and the standard drug treatment is the intravitreal (IVT)injection of anti-VEGF agents that must be frequently administered and are not adequatelyresponded by 40% DR patients. Oral administration of fenofibrate a PPAR agonist has shownrobust therapeutic effects on DR in type 2 diabetic patients; however oral fenofibrate has poorretinal absorption and IVT injection of fenofibrate is short-lived requiring frequent IVT injections.Here we propose a new method to provide sustained delivery of fenofibrate to effectively andsafely treat DR with 2 or less IVT injections/year significantly reducing the treatment burden ofdiabetic patients.
